Attention: The list of documents to be submitted is not exhaustive. In individual cases, further evidence may be required. When should I have an ID card? What this is and who offers it, read under Unlike in Germany, your foreign address is not entered in the identity card. The address given is “no main apartment in Germany”. title: Information according to Art. 13 and 14 Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (European General Data Protection Regulation - GDPR)
title_de: Information gemäß Art. 13 und 14 Verordnung ( EU ) 2016/679 (Europäische Datenschutz-Grundverordnung - DSGVO )
text: Information according to art. 13 and 14 Regulation (EC) EU ) 2016/679 (European General Data Protection Regulation) GDPR ) Artikel The Federal Foreign Office uses your personal data when processing your application. Personal data is any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person. To inform you about data processing and our obligation to provide information in accordance with Art. 13 and 14 GDPR We will inform you as follows: Who is responsible for the processing of my data and who is the data protection officer? Responsible for the processing of your personal data in accordance with Art. 4 No 7 GDPR are the Federal Foreign Office and the German missions abroad, which together form one federal hearing. Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany Avenue Ferdowsi 320-324 11365 Tehran info@tehe.diplo.de +98 (0) 21 3999 1670 Federal Foreign Office Werderscher Markt 1 D-10117 Berlin (Postal address: Federal Foreign Office, D-11013 Berlin). Tel: +49 30 18-17-0 Fax: +49 30-18-17-3402 Website: www.auswaertiges-amt.de Contact form The Federal Foreign Office Data Protection Commissioner can be contacted as follows: Federal Foreign Office Data Protection Commissioner Werderscher Markt 1 D-10117 Berlin Tel: + 49 30 18-17 0 Contact form What data does the mission abroad process when I apply for a visa and where does this data come from? The categories of personal data processed include the data requested under the visa application form. This usually includes in particular your family name, maiden name, first name, day and place with indication of the state of birth, sex, nationality(s), marital status, current address, telephone number, e-mail address, professional activity, details of the travel document (type of document, serial number, issuing state and authority, date of issue, period of validity), photograph and fingerprints. When applying for a national visa, further information is required, for example: information on the spouse / spouse / partner / partner, information on the children, information on the parents, previous stays, purpose of the intended stay, criminal history, expulsions, deportations, information on diseases. The mentioned data categories come from the information you provided in the visa procedure. What data does the mission process when I issue an invitation letter for someone applying for a visa and where does this data come from? The categories of personal data processed include the data requested under the applicant’s visa application form. This includes in particular your name and first name, address, e-mail address. The above-mentioned data categories come from the information provided by you in the invitation letter and by the applicant in the visa procedure. Why is my data collected and what happens if this does not happen? Your data is collected because this is necessary for the proper implementation of the visa procedure and is required by law. If you apply for a visa, it is your responsibility under Section 82 of the Residence Act (Residence Act). AufenthG ), provide the data necessary for processing the application and provide the necessary information. If your data is not provided, it may be that the request will be rejected with retention of the processing fee. For what purposes and on what legal basis will my data be processed? The processing of your personal data serves exclusively for the proper implementation of the visa procedure. The legal basis is Art. 6 para 1 lit. c) and e, para. 2 Regulation (EC) EU ) 2016/679 () GDPR ) iVm Regulation (EC) No 767/2008 (VIS Regulation) and Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Visa Code) including its annexes or §§ 72a ff. AufenthG ) and § 69 Residence Ordinance (AufenthV) as well as the Foreigners Central Register Act Implementing Ordinance (AZRG-DV), the Visa Warning File Act (VWDG) and If applicable, further special provisions or § 3 Federal Data Protection Act (Bundesdatenschutzgesetz) BDSG 2018. How long will my data be used? Your data will be deleted as soon as it is no longer necessary for the implementation of the visa procedure. As a rule, the deletion shall take place two years after the completion of the visa procedure, but no later than five years after the final decision on the visa applied for. Who gets my data? Your data will only be transmitted to third parties to the extent necessary for the proper implementation of the visa procedure. In this respect, your personal data may be transferred within the framework of the procedure to the respective competent German domestic authorities, to competent visa authorities and central authorities of other Schengen Member States or to the competent authorities at the place of your habitual residence. A transfer to recipients outside the European Union will only take place if this is done in accordance with Chapter V of the GDPR is permitted. If an external service provider is commissioned to carry out individual procedural steps in the visa procedure, your data will be collected by it or transmitted to it, as far as this is necessary to carry out the procedure. The external visa service provider shall receive applications for the issuance of visas as part of the mandate and forward them to B: In FIG. The Consulate General continue. The visa service provider processes as a processor within the meaning of the art. 28 GDPR personal data on behalf of the Federal Foreign Office. The person responsible for data processing remains the Federal Foreign Office (name and contact details of the person responsible see 1.). The processor ensures that the processing of your data in accordance with the requirements of the GDPR and the BDSG and the protection of your rights is guaranteed. For this purpose, the Federal Foreign Office has concluded a contract with the processor that meets the requirements of Art. 28, par. 3 GDPR contains and ensures the careful handling of your data. Your data, photograph, fingerprints and data relating to the decision on your application or a decision to annul, revoke or renew a visa in the application form for a Schengen visa (C visa) shall be entered in the Visa Information System (VIS) and stored there for a maximum period of five years; the visa authorities and the authorities responsible for checking visas at the external borders and in the Member States, as well as the immigration and asylum authorities in the Member States, shall have access to the data during those five years in order to verify that the conditions for legal entry into, and residence on, the territory of the Member States are met, to identify persons who do not or no longer fulfil those conditions, to examine an asylum application and to determine who is responsible for that examination. For the purposes of preventing, detecting and investigating terrorist offences and other serious criminal offences, designated authorities of the Member States and Europol shall also have access to those data under certain conditions. The competent authority of the Member State responsible for processing the data is: Bundesverwaltungsamt, D-50728 Köln, eu-zentrale-services@bva.bund.de. Further information on data processing in the VIS can be found here. The processing of data in the VIS is carried out in cooperation with the Federal Foreign Office. For this purpose, the Federal Office of Administration and the Federal Foreign Office have an agreement on joint responsibility in the processing of personal data according to art. 26 of the EU -Regulation 2016/679 () GDPR ) Closed. Further information on this can be found here. Requests for access to data relating to you in the VIS and the Member State which transmitted it to the VIS may: here submitted to the Federal Office of Administration. What data protection rights can I assert as a data subject? As a data subject, you have the following rights if the conditions are met: Right to information (Art. 15) GDPR ) Right to correction (Art. 16) GDPR ) Right to cancellation (Art. 17) GDPR ) Right to restrict processing (Art. 18) GDPR ) Right to data portability (Art. 20) GDPR ) Right to object to processing (Art. 21) GDPR ) Where can I complain? You have the right to complain to a data protection supervisory authority about the processing of your personal data. The supervisory authority responsible for the Federal Foreign Office and the German missions abroad is: The Federal Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information, Graurheindorfer Str. 153, 53117 Bonn, Tel: +49 228-997799-0, poststelle@bfdi.bund.de , www.bfdi.bund.de Zurück zu: "Service" Printing page leave whitespace for propper svg loading Sharing page WhatsApp Facebook X Mail upwards leave whitespace for propper svg loading

title: Inheritance matters
text: Inheritance matters Succession law and estate matters © Colourbox 01.12.2017 - Artikel In order to have an estate (for example, a property or account) in Germany, an inheritance certificate issued by a German estate court must usually be presented. The presentation of a legalized translation of an inheritance certificate issued by an Iranian law according to the Iranian court is usually not sufficient to be able to dispose of the property in Germany. The German certificate of inheritance is issued only on application, which includes, among other things, an affidavit and therefore certified must be. The application for an inheritance certificate can either be certified in Germany before the competent estate court or a German notary. In Iran, authentication at the Tehran Embassy can be carried out by an authorized consular officer. An insurance taken by an Iranian notary instead of Eides is not accepted by a German probate court. To prepare the application for the certificate of inheritance and the certificate, some information about the family and financial situation of the deceased is necessary. The embassy will draft a certificate of inheritance application based on your information and will arrange an appointment for the certificate with you. Please contact the Consular Department for further information on the procedure and certification by telephone or via the contact form. An appointment is necessary for advice in matters of succession law. Please book your appointment online: Appointment system of the Embassy of Tehran The notarization will be subject to a fee, which will be paid in cash. The amount of the fee is calculated in relation to the amount of the inheritance. The certified application must then be sent by you or your attorney along with the certified copies of the required civil status documents (and, if these are Iranian documents with legalized translation) to the competent probate court in Germany. If you do not wish to take up an inheritance to which you are entitled, you have the possibility under German law to refuse this inheritance within 6 weeks, if you are domiciled outside Germany within 6 months of knowledge of the inheritance by declaration to the German probate court. The declaration shall be made in a publicly certified form. The signature can be certified at the embassy’s legal and consular office. Please bring the letter of the probate court, the declaration of rejection and your passport with you during an audition at the counter of the embassy. After certification, you must send the certified declaration to the competent probate court in Germany. Central register of wills The Federal Chamber of Notaries maintains a “Central Register of Testaments” (ZTR). Every will or other document relevant to the succession (for example, inheritance agreement) must be reported for entry in the central register of the Federal Chamber of Notaries. In the event of death, the finding of a possibly. Testaments are facilitated and the probate court can decide through the innovation more quickly and as desired by the testator on the distribution of the probate. Documents already in custody are gradually digitized and reported to the Federal Chamber of Notaries for entry into the ZTR. Private wills, which are still kept at home, cannot be included in the ZTR. Further information can be found on the ZTR homepage: www.zentralestestamentsregister.de Decree of inheritance The forms necessary for this can be found here . Please note the instructions for the appointment booking. All information on this page and in the leaflets is based on findings and assessments of the message at the time of writing. However, no guarantee can be given for the completeness and correctness, in particular because of changes that have occurred in the meantime. 22.11.2017 Artikel Booking date Appointment system Embassy Tehran Zurück zu: "Startseite" Printing page leave whitespace for propper svg loading Sharing page WhatsApp Facebook X Mail upwards leave whitespace for propper svg loading

title: Iran: Travel and Security Advice: Travel Warning
text: Iran: Travel and Security Advice: Travel Warning Stand - 17.07.2026 (Unverändert gültig seit: 23.06.2026) Recent amendments: Current – Security situation in the region Editorial changes Current situation Security - Travel Warning Nature and climate Travel information Entry and customs Health Country information about your country of travel Further information for your trip Info Situations can change and develop quickly. We recommend: - Subscribe to our Newsletter Or use ours. App “Safe Travel”. - Register in our Crisis prevention list. Observe the instructions in Advisor for emergency preparedness and the Federal Office for Civil Protection and Disaster Assistance. - Please note Then, they say, COVID-19 . Take care of an adequate travel medical insurance. Ask them beforehand. Authorities/Embassies of your country of travel on the currently applicable, binding entry requirements as well as with your tour operator/air/ship/railway company in accordance with the applicable transport regulations. - Track your travel planning/while traveling, news and weather reports. - Pay attention to our Exclusion of liability and the reference to Content from other providers. Current situation Travel to Iran is warned. German citizens are asked to leave Iran. There is a risk of arbitrary arrests. The German Embassy in Tehran’s support for German citizens in Iran is currently extremely limited. Security situation in Iran At the end of February 2026, military confrontations between the USA Israel and Iran. Since then, there have been air strikes on various targets in Iran and attacks by Iran and pro-Iran militias on various targets in the region, Among others civilian facilities such as hotels, airports and office and residential buildings. Since the announcement of the ceasefire between the USA In early April 2026, Israel and Iran repeatedly launched mutual attacks on military and civilian targets in the region. Several parts of Iran were also hit by air strikes in June 2026. On June 17, 2026, the USA Iran has signed a memorandum of understanding to end the military conflict. There is still a risk that the security situation in the region will worsen and that there will be renewed hostilities, military strikes against states in the region and air traffic closures. In addition, an increased abstract threat to terrorist attacks continues unabated. Please note the applicable travel warning and departure request! Register in the Crisis prevention list the Federal Foreign Office or check whether your data stored there is up-to-date. Also unlock the emergency notification function via SMS. If you have left Iran in the meantime, please note this there as well. Be sure to follow the instructions of the local security forces, follow news in media closely Avoid large-scale demonstrations and other gatherings of people. See also our detailed information under “Security – Terrorism” and weigh your individual risk very carefully. Practice extreme restraint when shooting photos and videos, and in particular, do not take pictures of facilities that have been hit by bombs, missiles, drone strikes and have damage. There is a risk of arrests by the Iranian security forces, see Security – Photography and Filming . As a precautionary measure, ensure adequate storage (water, food, medicine, fuel); lb Always fully use mobile phones and other means of communication and external batteries . EU Sanctions Since October 2024 EU Sanctions against Iranian airlines Iran Air, Mahan Air and Saha Airlines in force. Existing flight connections to the EU were terminated immediately after publication of the decision. Persons and entities who are EU Sanctions may be imposed on nationals of EU -Member State neither directly nor indirectly funds, e.g. as part of the payment of an air ticket, or economic resources are provided or benefit. Violations of the provision ban are punishable in Germany according to § 18 AWG. Arbitrary arrests and prosecutions German nationals face a real risk of being arbitrarily arrested, interrogated and sentenced to long prison terms. In particular, dual nationals who hold Iranian citizenship alongside German citizenship are at risk. Recently, there have been numerous arbitrary arrests of foreign nationals. Therefore, travel to Iran is warned. Arrests and convictions can be made at any time on fabricated pretexts such as espionage charges. They can act as a means of political pressure and thus expose innocent victims to long imprisonment under harsh conditions. Statements critical of Iran, even before the trip and, for example, on social media, can be monitored by the Iranian side and be a reason for prosecution. Individual travelers may be subjected to espionage charges, such as photography. Iranian authorities deny German-Iranian dual-state citizens their right to consular assistance and make such assistance by the German Embassy in Tehran impossible. Detention conditions are very harsh; medically necessary medication or medically necessary medical treatment is not always provided. When travelling, there is a risk that baggage will be thoroughly searched and, if necessary, confiscated. Electronic means of communication such as mobile phones, notebooks and tablets can be subjected to in-depth investigations and the data read out can be used as a pretext for criminal charges. Individual travellers are particularly at risk, for example by motorcycle, bicycle or camper, because the data in electronic maps and navigation systems or entered routes, especially for planning off-road trips, can be considered by the Iranian security authorities as an indication of spying and espionage attempts. Restricted areas are usually not clearly marked. Conspicuous behavior or the use of technical devices such as eye-catching cameras, GPS devices and drones can also lead to inspections and quickly to espionage charges. The use of drones is generally prohibited. (see below) Security – “Photography and Filming” ) Similarly, the mere proximity to a military area, a nuclear installation or other sensitive facilities may be sufficient for arrest and conviction. Criminal laws are vaguely worded and can cover a variety of everyday behaviors without the need to be visible to those affected. Already private statements or the sharing, commenting or likening of posts on social media can constitute offences such as “espionage”, “terrorism” or so-called “Corruption on Earth” and thus lead to high prison sentences or even the death penalty. The same applies to personal records such as videos and photos on mobile phones, diary entries and other notes, as well as private contacts regularly consulted by the Iranian courts. Even people who have been able to enter and leave without problems in the past can be arbitrarily arrested on long-term or new charges. Long prison sentences under harsh conditions are possible; in the case of more serious allegations, the imposition of corporal punishment or the death penalty. Legal representation is cost-intensive and can reach five-digit euros. There is a travel warning for Iran, refrain from traveling to Iran. In particular, refrain from “adventure tourism” by bicycle, motorcycle, motorhome/camper – there is a particularly high risk of arbitrary arrest. In particular, do not travel to Iran if you have been critical of the country and the political system in the past. Be aware that not only in Iran, but also in Germany expressed opinions and actions in Iran can be perceived as critical and can therefore lead to criminal consequences there. This already includes participation in demonstrations critical of the regime in Germany, the mere sharing or liking of a foreign contribution critical of the government or the possession of videos or photos critical of the government on the mobile phone. Delete political photos and documents before entering the country. Note that phone calls to and from Iranian phone numbers can be tapped. In Iran, stay away from political events, rallies and basically from larger gatherings of people. Circumvent military areas as well as nuclear facilities extensively. In the event of arrest, carry absolutely necessary medication in sufficient quantity on the body for several days. In the event of a prolonged arrest, make arrangements for family law matters (e.g. custody of your children or care of family members in need of care) and provide: If applicable, Powers of attorney for the deputy performance of your private and professional obligations. Observe the instructions under Security - Domestic situation and Arbitrary arrests and prosecutions and Entry and customs - German-Iranian dual state . Observe also the instructions under Entry and customs on immigration control and refusals. Dual State The Federal Foreign Office emphasises the special risks for dual-state nationals of visiting Iran. Foreign nationals who: from the perspective of the Iranian authorities Iranian citizenship can only enter Iran with their Iranian passport. Iranian authorities consider the application for an Iranian passport and entry as a commitment to Iranian citizenship and They use it as a pretext to deny Germans their right to consular assistance through the German Embassy. See also: Security – domestic situation and Entry and customs - German-Iranian dual state . Dual nationals, including Iranian nationals, are particularly exposed to interrogations and/or arrests without any reasonable cause. There are more and more cases in which dual nationals are prevented from leaving the airport. an exit ban is imposed, also against persons resident in Germany. Family members of detainees are also regularly put under heavy pressure. Please note the applicable travel warning and exit request for all German nationals in Iran. Please note that the responsible German mission abroad can provide consular assistance to a very limited extent and cannot protect you from serious criminal prosecution. This is especially true if you use your Iranian passport when entering. Security - Travel Warning Travel to Iran is warned. German citizens are asked to leave Iran. There is a risk of arbitrary arrests. The German Embassy in Tehran’s support for German citizens in Iran is currently extremely limited. Terrorism In Iran, incidents with a terrorist background occur irregularly, especially in border regions. Particularly affected are the provinces of Kurdistan and Sistan-Baluchistan, the east of the province of Kerman and the border areas with Iraq, Pakistan and Afghanistan. In 2024, the attack on a funeral service at the tomb of General Kassem Soleimani in the city of Kerman (Southeast Iran) claimed many deaths and several hundred (severely) injured. Other Attacks in other provinces or in the capital Tehran cannot currently be ruled out. Two attacks on a Shiite shrine in the city of Shiraz killed at least 16 people in 2022 and 2023. The Iranian authorities have for some time increased the general security measures in the border area with Iraq and Pakistan, but also in the capital Tehran. There is a considerable risk of kidnapping and attack in eastern Kerman province and Sistan-Baluchistan province, as well as in Iran’s border areas with Pakistan and Afghanistan in general, especially in the triangle between the cities of Zabol, Bam and Chabahar. The situation is more stable in the port city of Chabahar itself. However, attacks have so far not been directed against foreign nationals. In the province of Sistan-Baluchistan (southeast, border with Pakistan/Afghanistan), there are regular conflicts between Iranian security forces and armed groups. Freedom of movement is restricted and there are increased security and personal controls. Repeatedly, foreign nationals were detained in the region and subjected to prolonged interrogations. In some cases, a further journey was only possible with Iranian police escort. This happened against the backdrop of years of frequent armed attacks on Iranian security forces in the region. In the province of Kurdistan and the province of West Azerbaijan, which is also inhabited by Kurds, there have been repeated attacks against security forces, judicial personnel and clergy. In this context, security forces have once again stepped up their actions against Kurdish separatist groups and checkpoints. Since 2015, according to Iranian figures, there have been repeated arrests in Khuzestan province and in other parts of the country, including Tehran, of people associated with the so-called Islamic State, who are said to have planned terrorist attacks in Iran. Please note the applicable travel warning and exit request for all German nationals in Iran. For the border regions with Iraq and Pakistan, always note the current situation in neighboring countries. Be especially attentive in busy places and on special occasions. Observe them. worldwide safety statement. Domestic situation See also Current. Since the death of the young Iranian Jina Mahsa Amini in September 2022, the security situation in the country has been tense. The demonstrations of late December/early January 2026 resulted in a very large number of deaths and injuries. There may still be (spontaneous) protests due to the continued repression by the security forces. Police and security forces can use force against demonstrators. This applies in particular to provinces with a high proportion of ethnic-religious minorities. e.g. Sistan-Beluchistan, Kurdistan. In the spatial environment of demonstrations, protesters and even uninvolved foreign nationals can be arbitrarily arrested. Communication services are largely restricted (in particular mobile Internet, Instagram, VPNs). In the past, German travelers who had close contact with Iranian nationals in the context of couchsurfing or other private overnight arrangements were arrested for violating Muslim morals and/or suspected security threats. Close contact between foreign and Iranian nationals can also put the Iranian nationals involved in serious danger. Entry and stay in Iran for a purpose other than that specified in the Iranian visa may be punishable, see Travel information – Special behavioural information. Please note the applicable travel warning and exit request for all German nationals in Iran. The security situation can change quickly and unpredictably throughout the country. If you are still in the country, be very careful and avoid large-scale demonstrations and gatherings. Avoid intensive contact with Iranian nationals. They can put themselves and others in serious danger. Avoid couchsurfing or other private overnight arrangements organized through social networks. Use your visa exclusively for the purpose stated therein. Visa abuse can have serious consequences up to arrest and conviction or deportation. Take photographs and film only with very little restraint and only with the permission of the recorded persons; Do not use drones, see Security – Photography and Filming . Avoid making statements about political conditions and events in Iran. During your stay, follow the current political situation and inform yourself about current developments via the local media or in your accommodation. Follow the instructions of local security forces. Find out about Iranian holidays. Be especially careful with the following data: 3. January - Death of General Kassem Soleimani 11. February – Revolution Day Ghods Day - (annual change of exact date) 3. June Death of Imam Khomeini July Ashoora mourning period with nationwide mourning processions (annual change of exact date) 16. September - Death of Mahsa Amini 4. November - Occupation of the US Embassy 7. December - so-called Student Day. Border region with Afghanistan and Pakistan The situation on the Iranian-Afghan border is confusing. In particular, arrests, detentions and deportations to Afghanistan are possible for persons who have Afghan nationality or other roots in the region. This also applies if you travel with a German passport. Even before the protests following the death of Mahsa Amini, the security situation in the border region with Pakistan, especially in the Sistan-Balochistan province, was extremely tense. The same applies to the security situation in Iran’s border areas with Afghanistan. Observe the instructions under Security and the applicable Travel warning for Afghanistan. Observe the instructions under Security, entry and customs and the Partial travel warning for Pakistan. The German Embassy in Tehran does not issue accompanying letters for visa applications to enter Afghanistan or to enter Pakistan by land. Entry and customs – Onward travel to neighbouring countries . Photography and filming In many public buildings, photography and filming are completely forbidden, such as in Tehran-Imam Khomeini Airport (IKA). The same applies to landscape photographs in certain regions. When and where bans apply is often not apparent. Photographing and filming even supposedly harmless places can lead to arrests and prosecutions on charges of espionage – See Currents . Under no circumstances should the following be photographed or filmed: State institutions or buildings or government vehicles Military installations or manoeuvres Nuclear power plants, refineries, oil/gas production facilities, drilling platforms gatherings or demonstrations Staff of official authorities (e.g. police or military) Embassy buildings Critical (supply) infrastructure such as electricity or waterworks Individuals without their express consent Avoid taking photos and videos in airports, train stations and similar buildings. Do not use drones, even in remote areas outside cities. Photograph and film only very reservedly. They can always be confronted with suspected espionage. Several years in prison are possible. Landmines In the areas on the Iranian-Iraqi border (especially in the western Iranian provinces of Kermanshah, Khuzestan, West Azerbaijan, Kurdistan and Ilam), landmines are expected as a result of the Iran-Iraq war (1980-1988). In the past, there were repeated incidents that often ended fatally. Uninhabited areas should be avoided in these regions, paved roads should not be abandoned under any circumstances. Avoid uninhabited areas in the border region with Iraq and do not leave the paved roads. There is a risk of landmines. Crime The crime rate is low overall in international comparison, but there are always pickpockets and handbag robbery, especially by passing motorcyclists. Mobile telephones are also snatched from the road as well as from vehicle windows as they pass by. There are cases where alleged security personnel carry out personal checks. Valuables are retained during such “control” and thus stolen. Vehicle thefts affect in particular those with all-wheel drive, such as off-road vehicles. In the past, there have been individual cases of sexual harassment, especially in crowds and remote areas. Avoid traveling at night or alone in remote areas. Be especially attentive in larger crowds such as at airports, train stations, in the subway, on the bus and pay attention to your valuables. Keep money, IDs, driver’s license and other important documents safe; Store If applicable, electronic copies/photos. This makes it easier to issue a replacement document in case of theft or loss. Always carry a copy of your passport and e-visa. Leave your passport and visa in a safe place during the day. Take only the cash needed for the day and no unneeded valuables. Keep windows and doors closed on car trips and prefer pre-ordered taxis over those on the road. Find out about the Iranian concept of “Taroof” – do not accept every polite offer of hospitality without understanding what is behind it. Be skeptical of unfamiliar emails, phone calls, profit notifications, offers and requests for assistance from alleged acquaintances. Do not share personal data, but make sure you If applicable, personally of credibility or contact the police. Nature and climate Iran is in a seismically very active zone. Earthquakes of different magnitudes are expected in most parts of the country. The climate is continentally dry with mild winters, except for the mountains to the north. Only on the Gulf Coast is it hot. The country is suffering from a prolonged drought; There are limited water supplies for the big cities. Temporary interruptions in the drinking water supply have therefore increased. In the mountains there can be avalanches, landslides and stone fall. Especially in the winter season, caution is required. Sudden heavy rainfall can lead to extensive flooding and disruption of travel. In some parts of the country, dust and sandstorms can occur all year round. Regularly follow weather reports and water supply information in Iran. Ensure a sufficient supply of drinking and useful water. Always stay on the designated paths and slopes and do not leave them. Think of the avalanche and landslide danger in the mountains. Stay away from river banks during heavy rain. Always observe bans, signs and warnings as well as the instructions of local authorities. Become aware of behavioral cues in earthquakes of the German Georesearch Centre trusted. Travel information See also Current situation Responsible mission abroad Due to the security situation, the staff of the German Embassy in Tehran were temporarily transferred from Iran and are currently unable to provide consular assistance. Infrastructure/Transport International flights from Tehran are handled via Imam Khomeini Airport (IKA), located outside the city. The centrally located Mehrabad Airport (THR) is only used for national flights. Several Iranian airlines have been which: EU -Air Security List They do not meet international safety standards. The number of road deaths, especially among motorcyclists (and their passengers), has been consistently high in Iran for years. The behaviour of many road users is unpredictable. Many vehicles are poorly maintained (lighting, brakes, tires, seat belts are often not available), which further increases the risk of accidents. At Iranian filling stations, usually only petrol and super in lower quality and low octane is available. Diesel is only available at a few gas stations and only outside the big cities, as no private diesel cars are driven in Iran. The quality of the diesel is often poor and some filling stations refuse to sell to private foreign drivers. As a rule, a separate fuel card is required, which should already be purchased at border crossing. When travelling by air to or within Iran, note the EU Air Safety List. Be careful on the road, especially as a pedestrian. Do not use two-wheelers, even as riders. When driving, watch for road elevations to reduce speed (speed bumps). Do not travel to Iran with military vehicles (even with former military trucks converted into motorhomes or similar). Be sure to pay attention to signs about military restricted areas, even if certain roads on maps may be marked as passable. Avoid desert tours. Driving licence Driving in Iran is possible up to six months with the German driving license. The international driving license is only valid in conjunction with the national German driving license. LGBTIQ Homosexual acts are punishable (as well as generally sexual acts outside marriage). In some cases, homosexual acts are punishable by the death penalty. Observe them. general instructions for: LGBTIQ . Boat excursions/specialities in the Strait of Hormuz From boat excursions off the west coast of the United Arab Emirates () UAE ) and into the Strait of Hormuz is strongly discouraged. Specific behavioural features Current Iranian laws and moral values, especially Islamic dress codes, should be respected. Legal specificities. Photo and video recordings should only be taken very restrictively and only with the permission of the persons involved, the use of drones is basically prohibited, see Security – Photography and Filming . Foreign nationals have also been charged and convicted for their statements on current political issues via telephone, social media and similar, see also Current – Arbitrary arrests and prosecutions , and Legal specificities . Many websites, apps and especially social media e.g. Facebook) and some email providers are blocked in Iran and can only be used via VPN. The use of unauthorized VPNs is punishable. Iranian nationals are prohibited from contacting numerous Western organizations and media. They were also asked not to maintain contacts with foreign nationals, foreign embassies and organisations working with them “beyond the normal level”. Arrests of both German and Iranian nationals can be the result. The validity of the Iranian visa should be respected. If it expires during the stay in Iran, the responsible foreigners police can extend it. If the visa has already expired, an exit is not possible. In this case, the Iranian foreigners police must be visited and possibly a fine be paid. The required exit visa can then be set up. Further information about the exit visa can be found on the Website of the German Embassy in Tehran to be found. Be sure to respect current laws and moral values, especially Islamic dress codes, see also Legal specificities. Do not exceed the validity of your visa. Use your visa exclusively for the purpose of entry specified in the visa. In the case of visa abuse, passport deprivation, detention or deportation can threaten. Be aware of possible monitoring of technical communication channels and do not send photos or travel reports related to current political developments. The same applies to SMS and phone calls. Be prepared for blocked access to many foreign websites and apps. Do not use “couchsurfing” or other private accommodation offered via social networks. Both you and your Iranian host face passport deprivation, trial and/or detention. Ramadan The date of the Islamic fasting month varies from year to year and is not the same as the dates in the Arab world. During this period of religious contemplation, members of the Muslim faith are required to fast, that is, to refrain from food, drinks, smoking and other sensual pleasures from sunrise to sunset. In public, eating, drinking and smoking is also forbidden for other or non-believers during the day. Exceptions apply to pregnant women, the sick, children and travellers. Expect restrictions in everyday life (closure of many restaurants during the day, reduced working hours with authorities) and increased sensitivity in religious matters as well as in matters relating to respect for Islamic traditions. Respect the religious feelings of the local population and treat fasting with respect, even if you are a different or non-believer. Legal specificities Iranian authorities do not recognise dual citizenship. They treat people who also have Iranian citizenship as if they were exclusively Iranian citizens. Consular access through the German Embassy is regularly denied to this group of people, see Current situation . Acts that are punishable under Western legal understanding are also punishable in Iran. In addition, actions that are unproblematic according to Western legal understanding but can violate Muslim values (for example, dancing) can be punished. The penalties imposed are often severe and often incompatible with Western legal understanding. Physical punishments (for example, lashes) are possible. The prison conditions are very harsh. For serious drug offences, murder, armed robbery, serious financial offences and certain sexual acts (sexual intercourse outside marriage, same-sex intercourse) the death penalty may be imposed. Offences against the Narcotics Act are punishable with long prison sentences even in the smallest quantities and for any type of drug. Possession, consumption and importation of alcohol are prohibited. For women (from nine years old) and also men, the Islamic dress regulations apply: The coat for women (called Manteau) must cover the female body forms. Hair and neck must be covered by a headscarf. The arms and legs must reach up to the wrists or ankles are covered. Men have to wear long pants. In religious places like mosques, long shirts must be worn. There are more and more checks on correct clothing, especially in religiously important cities such as Mashhad or Qom, but again and again in Tehran. When dealing with Iranian women or men in public, police checks must be expected. Even just staying near military or nuclear facilities can lead to serious misunderstandings and even espionage charges. There is a risk of arbitrary arrest, interrogation or sentences of several years in prison. The use of VPN networks is controlled, that of an unauthorized VPN network is punishable. Violations of the Convention on the Protection of Species are severely prosecuted by the Ministry of the Environment and can be punished with prison sentences of up to three years. Be sure to observe the laws and rules applicable to the relationship between man and woman. Do not use “couchsurfing” or other private accommodation offered via social networks. Both you and your Iranian host may face passport deprivation, trial or detention. Refrain from importing or using drones. Avoid using VPN services. Behave particularly cautiously when staying near security objects, see Current. Avoid stays in the immediate vicinity of locations of safety-protected facilities in Buschher, Natanz, Qom and the corresponding objects in the vicinity of Arak and Isfahan. Take photographs and film only very reservedly and only with the permission of the recorded persons, see Security – Photography and filming . Do not export animals or plants. Money/Credit cards Local currency is the rial (IRR), but prices are often quoted in Toman (100,000 rial equals 10,000 toman, spoken often as 10 toman). Before a trade agreement, inquire which currency is meant. Due to the sanctions against Iran, the country is not connected to international payments. Credit cards and other foreign debit cards cannot be used. Take enough cash (IRR, EUR or USD) for the entire stay. There is no way to withdraw money or obtain it elsewhere. Entry and customs See also Current situation Entry and customs regulations for German nationals may change at short notice without the Federal Foreign Office being informed in advance. Therefore, please inquire in advance with the representations of your target country. Only there you will receive legally binding information and/or information beyond these notices about the entry and customs regulations of your travel country. The German customs informs about the currently applicable Customs provisions when entering Germany. Travel documents Entry is possible for German nationals with the following documents: Passport: Yes Provisional passport: Yes Identity card: No Provisional identity card: No Child passport: Yes Comments: Travel documents must remain valid for at least six months at the time of entry. Visa German citizens need a visa to enter the country. providing information on the visa which: Iranian Embassy in Berlin. The visa is not glued into the passport, but is issued as an electronic visa that is carried in the form of a printout. This also eliminates the affixing of an entry stamp in the passport. Keep your passport and visa safe throughout your stay. For non-tourist stays in Iran, the correct visa (for example, journalist visa, work visa) must be applied for. In case of violation of Iranian entry requirements, criminal prosecution and disproportionate penalties must be imposed. Among others several years of imprisonment. If the validity of the visa is exceeded or if the passport is lost, considerable difficulties are to be expected. The issuance of an exit visa by the Iranian authorities can take several days to weeks. If the visa period is exceeded, penalties are usually due to the Aliens Police and a processing fee for the exit visa. Inform them on the Website of the German Embassy in Tehran the procedure for the loss or expiration of your German passport and the application steps for an Iranian exit visa. Visa before entry Before entering the country, an online visa can ( e-Visa ) to be applied for entry into Iran. Visa on arrival There is no reliable information as to whether the Visa on arrival process has been working reliably since the COVID 19 pandemic. The use of “Visa on arrival” is strongly discouraged. Health insurance For the duration of the stay in Iran, an Iranian or German accident and health insurance is necessary. Often a health insurance certificate is required, which COVID-19 -Included disease. Please contact the Iranian Embassy in Germany to find out the exact requirements for your health insurance abroad. Entry and exit border crossings Foreign nationals can generally enter through the country’s international airports in accordance with the applicable requirements. The border crossings to Pakistan are located in the province of Sistan-Beluschistan, where clashes between demonstrators and security forces as well as gang crime (especially drug smuggling) accompanied by raids and firearm use are recorded. A crossing by ship from Iranian ports on the Persian Gulf to the United Arab Emirates is strongly discouraged, as it can be illegal ferry and cargo connections. All entry, customs and foreign exchange forms should be filled out carefully and accurately. Passengers travelling by land with their own vehicle need Iranian car insurance for their vehicle. Observe them. Partial travel warning for Pakistan and the Travel warning for Afghanistan. Onward travel to Afghanistan and Pakistan is not supported in any way. Border closures continue to occur on both sides due to security incidents and unrest. Inform yourself in advance and when entering the country at the border about the conclusion of a corresponding car insurance for your vehicle Check with the Iranian authorities responsible for the border crossing point about the exact entry requirements. Please also inform yourself before departure about possible requirements for departure. or the rules for transit to the authorities of the respective countries. Onward travel to neighbouring countries See also Partial travel warning to Pakistan. To apply for a visa for some neighboring countries of Iran () e.g. Pakistan, Turkmenistan) in Iran is often required by the relevant foreign missions a consular certificate confirming the traveller’s passport data. Travel to the USA After stays in Iran Travel to Iran can take place later in the USA impact. Further information can be found in the Travel and safety advice USA . Minors No specific provisions for the entry of minors are known. Observe the custody provisions of Iranian law that deviate from German law and inform yourself in advance about the applicable legal situation and the resulting consequences. Observe If applicable, The guidance for an Declaration of consent for minors . German-Iranian dual state See also Current – Arbitrary arrests and prosecutions Travel to Iran is warned. German nationals face a real risk of being arbitrarily arrested, interrogated and sentenced to long prison terms. In particular, dual nationals who hold Iranian citizenship alongside German citizenship are at risk. Persons who hold both German and Iranian citizenship so-called Dual nationals are treated exclusively as Iranian nationals by the Iranian authorities. This means that consular assistance by the German Embassy Tehran is not possible in emergencies. Iranian citizenship cannot be removed, and Iran automatically considers children of Iranian parents to be Iranian. An Iranian nationality can also be present without an Iranian passport or Iranian birth documents and also affect people who have had no contact with Iran themselves. The characteristic of an Iranian nationality suspected by Iranian authorities is, for example, name or place of birth. Dual-country nationals, including Iranian nationals, are particularly exposed to interrogation and/or detention without reasonable cause, including when entering Iran. There is an increasing number of cases of imposition of exit bans, Among others for non-performing military service, as well as checking mobile phones, camera and PC. Also a criminal prosecution of political activities in Germany () e.g. Participation in demonstrations critical of the regime in Germany, dissemination of posts critical of the regime via social media, and imprisonment and conviction in Iran cannot be ruled out. In 2023, people of Iranian origin living in Western countries who had participated in demonstrations critical of the regime were arrested, prosecuted and tortured to make confessions in order to deter protesters. Under Iranian law, the cohabitation of a man and a woman in a conjugal union without marriage is punishable. Dual-country nationals whose marriage is not recognized in Iran may face criminal consequences upon entry. In this case, Iranian birth certificates and passports must be requested in Iran for children who have joined the company before leaving Iran is possible. This can lead to considerable difficulties and time delays. Carry with you your valid Iranian passport to enter and exit Iran. Note that consular assistance from the German Embassy in Tehran is significantly limited or impossible. In particular, the embassy cannot protect you from possible criminal prosecution. If the validity of your Iranian passport has expired or your passport has been revoked by the Iranian authorities, the departure is only after a new Iranian passport has been issued. Return of your passport possible by the Iranian authorities. A German passport is not sufficient for departure from Iran if Iranian citizenship exists at the same time; You also need an Iranian passport. To enter Germany, carry a valid German passport with you. In the event that you want to return to Germany without a valid German passport, you should check with your airline before starting your trip whether your transport is also possible with an identity card or an expired passport. German-Iranian military service dual state With the beginning of the Iranian calendar year (“Nowruz”, March 21), in which a man turns 18, he becomes a conscript in Iran and has to perform active military service for 24 months. After reaching the age of 50, a man is exempt from military service; If applicable, However, he must perform military service until the age of 60. There is an obligation to register for military service (all Iranians living abroad must report to the Iranian mission responsible for them without request). Under certain conditions, Iranians abroad can be granted entry into Iran once a year without compulsory military service. Details must be requested from the competent Iranian mission abroad. Please note that as a dual-state male, you may receive an exit ban until you complete military service in Iran (or obtain an exemption). Information for German wives of Iranian nationals To the knowledge of the German Embassy in Tehran, the German wife automatically acquires Iranian citizenship through an effective marriage to an Iranian under Iranian law. Legally binding information on the acquisition of Iranian citizenship can only be provided by the competent Iranian authorities. German citizenship is not lost in an automatic acquisition of citizenship. In individual cases, it may happen that the Iranian authorities upon entry or when issuing the so-called Shenasnameh ( civil status document) for the German wife retain the German passport of the persons concerned. In this case, the German Embassy in Tehran should be contacted immediately. Since under Iranian law the husband has the right of residence for both his wife and the joint children, the Iranian husband can refuse to leave his wife and the joint children. Wife and children need the consent of their husband to leave the country again. Especially for children travelling with them, the risk of a possible exit ban with all its consequences must be carefully considered. The Iranian husband may impose an exit ban as long as the marriage continues under Iranian law. This is also possible after a divorce outside Iran, as long as the foreign divorce has not been officially registered by the Iranian authorities and entered in the Shenasnamehs ( civil status documents) of the parties involved. A departure of the woman without the consent of the former husband is not possible in this case until the registration and registration of the divorce in an Iranian court proceeding. Go before entering. If applicable, with this problem. If your passport is taken from you when you enter, take it immediately with you. German Embassy in Tehran Contact. Request: If applicable, Registration of the divorce at an Iranian mission abroad in good time before a trip to avoid delaying the return to Germany. Import requirements Cash with a value of up to EUR 10,000 may be imported without further explanation and executed with a value of up to EUR 5,000. Higher amounts must be declared. Do not introduce alcohol, pork and publications that could violate Iran’s very strict moral understanding. They may carry out a carpet up to six square metres and not more than 30 years old. The export of antiques (items older than 30 years) is only permitted with a permit from the Cultural Heritage Organization. For this purpose, visit the Cultural Heritage Organization of Tehran Province on presentation of an air ticket as proof of departure by prior appointment (tel. +98 21 667020614, extension 212, Imam Khomeini Str. 30 Tir Str., Iran-Bastan Museum). Entry by car It is unclear whether it is still possible to enter Iran with your own motor vehicle, see Current situation . Information is provided by the responsible Iranian mission abroad in Germany or the Iranian border authorities. Until now, a “carnet de passage” and adequate insurance coverage for the entire stay in Iran were required for entering the country. When entering with a diesel vehicle, there were increasing problems. Animals Imports of domestic animals shall be subject to an official veterinary certificate not older than 10 days attesting that the animal is healthy, has been vaccinated against rabies within the last six months, has been worn and has a chip. Recommended is the EU Pet passport. When entering the airport, a new official veterinary examination is usually carried out in addition to a check. Inquire Please contact the Iranian missions directly regarding the import of pets. Health Vaccination protection No compulsory vaccinations are required for direct entry from Germany. When entering a yellow fever area or staying for more than 12 hours in transit of a yellow fever area, all persons aged nine months and over must: Yellow fever vaccination evidence. Iran itself is not a yellow fever infection area. Make sure that you and your children receive the standard vaccinations The vaccination calendar of STIKO are up to date. Ensure full immunization against poliomyelitis (polio), see Poliomyelitis. As travel vaccinations, vaccination against hepatitis A, in the case of long stay or special exposure also against dengue fever, hepatitis B, typhoid fever and rabies are recommended. Dengue fever There are reports of autochthonous infections in individual provinces in Iran. Dengue viruses are caused by day-active Aedes Mosquitoes. The disease is usually accompanied by fever, rash and pronounced limb pain. Rarely, serious complications occur, especially in children incl. possible deaths. Overall, however, complications among travelers are rare. Chemoprophylaxis and specific therapy do not exist. Vaccination is available, see Dengue fever Protect yourself to avoid dengue fever in the context of a Protection against insects especially during the day consistently before mosquito bites. Seek advice on vaccination from tropical and/or travel medical professionals. Malaria Malaria is a serious disease transmitted by mosquitoes. After 4 years without local malaria cases, a local malaria transmission has been established again in 2022. Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax ) in parts of the provinces of Sistan-Beluchistan and Hormozgan. A map of malaria risk areas shows the DTG available. The risk of malaria is year-round and varies regionally. Year-round Medium Risk: Parts of Sistan-Balochistan provinces bordering Pakistan Year-round low risk: Rest of Sistan-Balochistan province and parts of Hormozgan and Kerman provinces Protect yourself consistently from mosquito bites at dusk and at night. Use mosquito repellents regularly and wear appropriate clothing. Use bed nets at night, see Protection from insects. When travelling to areas at medium risk of malaria that are more than 48 hours away from the nearest medical facility with malaria diagnosis and treatment facilities, it is recommended to carry appropriate medication for emergency self-treatment. Get medical advice on the selection of appropriate medications. HIV/AIDS Due to sexual contact, drug use (unclean syringes or cannulas) and blood transfusions, there is a high risk of HIV transmission, which is only low in Iran (prevalence below 0.1%). Always use condoms, especially for casual acquaintances. Diarrhea Diarrheal diseases are still relatively widespread in Iran. Diarrhea diseases are common travel diseases, see Diarrhea . Through appropriate food and drinking water hygiene, however, most diarrhea diseases can be avoided. In order to protect your health, you should therefore consider the following basic information: Drink only water of safe origin, never tap water. By purchasing bottled water with carbonic acid, a previously opened bottle can be more easily identified. On the way, also use drinking water for dishwashing and brushing your teeth. If bottled water is not available, use filtered, disinfected or boiled water. Cook or peel food yourself. Be sure to keep flies away from your food. Wash your hands with soap as often as possible, but always before food preparation and before eating. If possible, disinfect your hands with liquid disinfectant. Rabies Rabies is a deadly infectious disease caused by viruses transmitted by the saliva of infected animals or humans. Nationwide, there is a high risk of bite injuries from stray dogs and monkeys, see Rabies . Avoid contact with animals. Get advice and vaccination regarding a rabies vaccination. The vaccination series should be completed before travel. Also in the case of existing vaccination, seek medical attention immediately after contact with a potentially infected animal or human (bite, bumps of injured skin areas or saliva droplets on the mucous membranes of the mouth, nose and eyes). Leishmaniae Skin leishmaniasis in particular is common in rural areas. This is a sand flies-borne, parasitic disease with skin changes that usually occur weeks to months after the bite and persist for a long time. In the case of persistent, unclear fever flare-ups and spleen enlargement, the dangerous, generalized so-called “visceral” form. Protect yourself in the context of a Exposure prophylaxis Suitable for sandflying. Imagine yourself with non-healing skin ulcers or persistent flare-ups of fever in a tropical medicine experienced clinic. Geographical diseases Iran is currently a rather rare destination for trekking tourists who rise to high altitudes. Often the time is not enough for a stepwise height adjustment. Various forms of acute altitude sickness are possible, see altitude sickness. Before travelling to high altitudes (over 2,300 m), get individual advice from a medically experienced doctor before final travel planning. A travel health insurance that covers the mountain risk () e.g. A helicopter evacuation) is recommended. Intense sunlight, glare from snow and ice, strong wind, extreme cold and rough or unknown terrain pose further risks for travelers at high altitudes. Earthquakes or persistent precipitation can cause avalanches, murmurs and slippages of entire mountain slopes in vulnerable areas. There are no civilian air rescue companies for helicopter rescues in Iran, but only the possibility of an evacuation by the military, which is very bureaucratic and time-consuming. Depending on the location of the accident, the alarm can often be very delayed, the use of satellite telephones is strictly prohibited in Iran. Despite great time pressure due to obtaining permits from various government agencies, it can sometimes take several days, especially on weekends, until a search party or a helicopter is ready for use. Due to weather conditions, the aircraft are not always ready for use and can usually only take off early in the morning in a very narrow time window. The cost of a rescue must in any case be clarified before the start of the rescue operation and is usually first to be borne by the victim himself. Air pollution Traffic pressure is high in Tehran. Due to the additional exhaust gases from industry, increased smog pollution occurs in the metropolis of millions surrounded by mountains, especially in the winter months. Breathing problems and headaches are not uncommon. Find out about current air values e.g. over the World Air Quality Index. Medical services Specialised medical care, especially in emergencies or accidents, is in large parts of the country medical, hygienic, technical and organizational not at the height of the capital and not comparable to European standards. Language barriers can make communication more difficult, especially in rural areas. A reliably functioning rescue system is not everywhere in the cities. In Tehran, medical care in all disciplines is usually possible at a very high level. Even in the case of serious illnesses, therefore, only rarely a medical evacuation abroad, e.g. to Dubai, or an immediate repatriation may be considered. Sufficient health insurance coverage, including travel return insurance, is nevertheless urgently needed. Take out an international travel health and return insurance for the duration of your stay abroad. Take an individual travel pharmacy and protect it against high temperatures on the way. Before a trip, get personal advice from travel medical counselling centres, tropical or travel medical specialists and adjust your vaccination protection. Appropriate doctors can be found e.g. concerning: DTG . Please note in addition to the general also the medical liability exclusion Country information about your country of travel Here you will find addresses of responsible diplomatic missions and information on politics and bilateral relations with Germany. More Further information for your trip The global threat of terrorist attacks and kidnappings continues. In particular, the terrorist organisations “Al Qaeda” and “Islamic State” ( IS ) threaten with attacks against various countries and their nationals. The conflict in the Middle East as a result of the terrorist attacks against Israel on 7 July October 2023, there is an increased mobilization potential. Even if there are currently no concrete indications of a threat to German interests abroad, it cannot be excluded that German nationals or German institutions abroad will also be the target of terrorist acts of violence. Possible targets are especially places with a symbolic character. These include well-known attractions, government and administrative buildings, transport infrastructure (especially planes, trains, ships, buses), economic and tourism centers, hotels, markets, religious gatherings and generally larger crowds. The degree of terrorist threat varies from country to country. There is a risk of attack in particular in countries and regions where repeated attacks have already been carried out or where, in the absence of effective security measures, terrorists have the backing of the local population. More detailed information about the terrorist threat can be found in the country-specific travel and security advice. These are regularly reviewed and adjusted as necessary. The risk of being the victim of an attack remains relatively low compared to other risks associated with travel abroad, such as accidents, illnesses or common crime. Nevertheless, travelers should be aware of the danger. Behave in a security-conscious and situational manner. Before and during a trip, inform yourself carefully about the conditions in your country of travel. Follow the local and international media. Report suspicious events (such as unattended baggage in airports or train stations, suspicious behavior of people) to local police or security authorities. German nationals are recommended to sign on the Federal Foreign Office’s “Elefand” crisis preparedness list, regardless of the country and the duration of their stay abroad: Crisis prevention list Travel and security advice shall be based on the information available at the specified time and assessed as trustworthy by the Federal Foreign Office and shall be updated on an ongoing basis. References to special regulations abroad always concern only a few selected questions and merely provide assistance from the Federal Foreign Office, but they cannot replace information provided by the competent authorities of the respective state. Legal provisions can also change at any time without the Federal Foreign Office being informed. Please note that danger situations are often confusing and can change quickly. Therefore, information researched with the utmost care may be inaccurate or incomplete. The Federal Foreign Office therefore recommends that you contact your transport service provider and the relevant diplomatic or consular representation of the destination country before travelling. Please also note that the decision to start a trip is ultimately always your sole responsibility. A general guarantee for the correctness and completeness as well as a liability for any damages will not be assumed. The Federal Foreign Office strongly recommends that the recommendations contained in the travel and security advice be followed and that insurance cover be provided. e.g. a foreign health insurance cover with return insurance. In this context, it is noted that you will be charged costs for any necessary assistance measures under the Consular Law. In addition to the general disclaimer, please note: A guarantee for the correctness and completeness of the medical information as well as a liability for possible damages can not be assumed. All information can be seen depending on the individual travel conditions and does not replace the medical consultation or in-depth medical advice. Where applicable, information shall include: Id.R. for direct entry from Germany to a travel country and are especially tailored for longer stays on site. For shorter journeys and entries from third countries, derogations may apply. Please clarify with your health insurance or health insurance whether there is adequate health insurance coverage for your trip abroad, which also covers the costs of a rescue flight to Germany. Without adequate insurance coverage, necessary costs are incurred on site () e.g. Treatment or hospital costs, home flight) should be borne by the person concerned and can quickly eat up all your savings. It is common in many countries that doctors or the costs charged to hospitals must be paid before discharge or even advanced. Nor can it be excluded that the doctor/hospital will obtain a refusal of departure for the patient if the bill is not paid. Information on common infectious and tropical diseases Many travel souvenirs are subject to strict import rules. Inform yourself in time! Please inform yourself before the start of your trip about which travel souvenirs may not be introduced for species protection reasons. Quite a few tourists experience a nasty surprise on their return when the memorabilia is confiscated by customs or even followed by penalties. Even if an exotic souvenir impresses so much with its peculiarity and uniqueness - many animal and plant species from which such souvenirs are made are endangered in their stock or even threatened with extinction. These souvenirs are subject to strict import regulations. Customs and the Federal Office for Nature Conservation have the online portal for travelers. Species protection on holiday introduced for travellers. You can find out exactly which souvenirs can be imported into Germany from which countries. Please do not contribute to the illegal and harmful trade in wild animals and plants. Nature conservation organisations as well as environmental and customs authorities advise not to take any risks and, in principle, to refrain from souvenirs made from animals or plants. Instead, they recommend purchasing country-specific textiles, ceramics, metal and glass works or paintings. In principle, you should exercise caution when buying animal and plant products if you cannot trace their origin. So you do not harm the animal and plant world of your holiday country. Please also note our questions and answers on the subject of “safety instructions” FAQ . Exclusion of liability Planning and commencing international trips always under your own responsibility! Reise- und Sicherheitshinweise beruhen auf den zum angegebenen Zeitpunkt verfügbaren und als vertrauenswürdig eingeschätzten Informationen des Auswärtigen Amts und werden fortlaufend aktualisiert. Hinweise auf besondere Rechtsvorschriften im Ausland betreffen immer nur wenige ausgewählte Fragen und stellen lediglich eine Hilfestellung seitens des Auswärtigen Amtes dar, sie können eine Information durch die zuständigen Stellen des jeweiligen Staates jedoch nicht ersetzen. Gesetzliche Vorschriften können sich zudem jederzeit ändern, ohne dass das Auswärtige Amt hiervon unterrichtet wird. Bitte beachten Sie, dass Gefahrenlagen oft unübersichtlich sind und sich rasch ändern können. Auch daher können mit größter Sorgfalt recherchierte Informationen unzutreffend oder unvollständig sein. Das Auswärtige Amt empfiehlt deswegen, dass Sie sich vor Reiseantritt etwa an Ihren Transportdienstleister sowie die jeweils zuständige diplomatische oder konsularische Vertretung des Ziellandes wenden. Bitte beachten Sie auch, dass die Entscheidung über den Antritt einer Reise letztlich immer in Ihrer alleinigen Verantwortung liegt. Eine generelle Gewähr für die Richtigkeit und Vollständigkeit sowie eine Haftung für eventuell eintretende Schäden wird nicht übernommen. Das Auswärtige Amt rät dringend, die in den Reise- und Sicherheitshinweisen enthaltenen Empfehlungen zu beachten sowie einen entsprechenden Versicherungsschutz, z.B. einen Auslands-Krankenversicherungsschutz mit Rückholversicherung, abzuschließen. In diesem Zusammenhang wird darauf hingewiesen, dass Ihnen Kosten für erforderlich werdende Hilfsmaßnahmen nach dem Konsulargesetz in Rechnung gestellt werden.
