title: Legal and Consular Department
text: Legal and Consular Department Photo of a stamp © colourbox Artikel Important note The legal and consular department and the external service provider TLScontact are closed until further notice. Consular services can only be offered to a very limited extent. For individual questions, the message can be reached via the contact form. Information provided by the Legal and Consular Department To help you find information, we have compiled a questionnaire with typical questions from clients of the legal and consular department. The link under the respective short answer leads you to the corresponding page of the message homepage, where you can find further information on this topic. You can also read many more questions and detailed answers on the website of the Citizens Service of the Federal Foreign Office: FAQ – Citizen Service Stay information I am looking for a relative to stay in Iran. Can the message help me Finding the address ? Address determinations by the embassy are not possible, as there is neither the German comparable registration authorities nor a foreigners central register or comprehensive telephone directories in Iran. There is no reporting obligation in Iran. Application for a certificate How can I get a German Certificate of conduct request? A German certificate of conduct is issued by the Federal Office of Justice. The request must be sent to the BAfJ in certified form. The authentication of the signature can be carried out in the consular department of the embassy. Information for applying for a certificate of conduct Acquisition of documents I live in Germany and need an original of my Iranian “Shenasnameh” to get married in Germany. Can the message give me this Procurement of documents ? No. In principle, the embassy cannot obtain Iranian documents. The new certificate can usually be requested via an Iranian mission abroad in Germany. Furthermore, it is possible to hire an Iranian lawyer or relative on site with this task. German passports Ms Passport is no longer valid. Can I have it extended at the German Embassy or do I have to go back to Germany? After expiry of validity, a new passport must be applied for. This request can be made at the embassy. Passport and identity card I have my German Passport In Iran Lost . Now what do I have to do? How do I get back to Germany? If a German passport is lost, the passport holder is obliged to report this to the German authorities immediately. Please report the loss of your passport first to the Iranian police and have a loss log issued. With this loss log you can then report the loss of passport in the consular department of the embassy and apply for a new passport. Passport and identity card Authentication of German documents Translators in Tehran demand a stamp of embassy for translation into Persian. Can the Embassy confirm my German documents for a translation into Persian? No. The authenticity of German documents cannot usually be confirmed by the embassy. German documents to be used in Iran must be legalised by an Iranian mission abroad in Germany. Marriage in Iran Is my marriage in Iran also valid in Germany? What do I have to do for my marriage to be recognized? A marriage concluded in Iran is recognized in Germany if it was concluded under Iranian laws and registered with the Iranian authorities. A formal recognition of marriage by German authorities is not necessary. As a rule, a legalized German translation of the Iranian marriage certificate is sufficient as proof of marriage. If a spouse has German citizenship, it is possible to register the marriage in the German marriage register and apply for a German marriage certificate. More information about marriage in Iran inheritance I inherited a house/account in Germany. The bank now requires the submission of a Certificate of inheritance . How can I get this one? The application for a German certificate of inheritance must be notarized. Such deeds may be carried out in the Consular Department. Detailed prior advice and appointment agreement is absolutely necessary. Erbschaftsangelegenheiten An appointment is necessary for advice in matters of succession law. Please book your appointment online: Appointment system of the Embassy of Tehran Driving licence Can I have a new message Driving licence request? No. The message is not a driving license authority. Responsible for driving licence matters is always the Transport Office; at the last registration residence in Germany. Birth of a child in Iran I am a German citizen and mine Child was born in Iran Born . What do I have to do so that my child also gets a German passport? A child automatically acquires German citizenship at birth if a parent is in possession of German citizenship at birth. If the parents are not married to each other and only the father is German, a paternity recognition is first necessary to acquire German citizenship. Passport and identity card Wedding in Germany What do I have to do if I am an Iranian citizen in Germany Married Want to? To enter the wedding in Germany, an Iranian national requires a marriage visa. Before applying for the visa, the marriage must first be registered with a German registry office. To register with the registry office, your fiancée must submit a declaration of accession (sometimes also power of attorney). This declaration can be certified in the Consular Department. Certificates and attestations Dress rules for tourists Do I also have to wear a headscarf as a tourist in Iran? Yes. In Iran, wearing Islamic clothing is required by law. Women should always wear a headscarf and a roughly knee-length coat in public. Men should not wear shorts. Information from the Federal Foreign Office on Iran Embassy crisis list Can I also stay on the Crisis prevention list register the embassy if I am only in Iran for a short time, for example as a tourist? Yes. A registration on the “German list” of the embassy is also possible and useful for a short stay in Iran. Crisis prevention and emergencies Certificate of life My pension insurance requires me to Certificate of life . What is this and how can I get something like this? Pension insurance requires the presentation of a life certificate once a year, as the pension entitlement exists only until the end of life. As a rule, the pension recipients receive the corresponding form from the insurance company by post. With the form and your passport you must then Personal Speak at the message. Rentenangelegenheiten Legalization / Legalized Translation I live in Germany and need for German authorities a legalization of the German embassy of my Iranian marriage certificate / Shenasnameh. I have the original document with me. Is it possible to obtain the document for Legalisation Send by post to the embassy or is a copy sufficient? No. Legalisations can only be requested by speaking at the counter. Application by post is not possible. Those who do not live in Tehran can commission acquaintances or relatives to carry out legalization. The submission of a copy is not sufficient. For legalization, the original of the document must be presented. Legalisation of documents and copy-certificates List of sworn translators in Iran: https://sanam.ekfam.ir/Home/TranslatorList Identity card Can I have a new message Identity card request? For more information, see the link below: Passport and identity card Security advice, visa requirements for travel to Iran I will soon be travelling to Iran as a tourist. What do I need to consider regarding the security situation? Do I need a visa? In preparation for your trip to Iran, you should definitely read our travel and security advice on the Federal Foreign Office website carefully. There you will also find information about the visa conditions for German nationals. German citizens can also register their stay in Iran online. Registration is highly recommended, even for short stays. Crisis prevention and emergencies Travel and precautionary instructions Nationality I and my children are German citizens. My wife/husband has only Iranian citizenship so far. How can she/he get a German passport? Your spouse can only Naturalisation Receive a German passport. If you are resident in Germany, the local nationality authority is responsible for this. If you live in Iran, the Federal Office of Administration decides on naturalization from abroad. A prerequisite for such naturalisation is a demonstrably close bond with Germany and a public interest in naturalisation. According to the experience of the Federal Foreign Office, marriage to a German citizen alone is not enough. An appointment is required for advice on matters of nationality law. Please book your appointment online: Appointment system of the Embassy of Tehran Homepage of the Federal Office of Administration Visa for travel to the USA I am a German citizen, but I do not yet have a biometric passport with fingerprints. Can I still get my passport into the USA Travelling? German citizens participate in the Visa Waiver Program of the USA Part. For visa-free entry, only the regular (bordeaux red) passports are entitled, even if they do not yet contain a chip. Please find out more about the Visa Waiver Program and the other entry requirements in the travel instructions of the Federal Foreign Office for the USA travel and safety advice for the USA All information on this page is based on findings and assessments of the message at the time of writing. However, no guarantee can be given for the completeness and correctness, in particular because of changes that have occurred in the meantime. title: Legalisation of documents and copy-certificates
text: Legalisation of documents and copy-certificates Stamp © Liesson/photothek.net Artikel Info An appointment is necessary to apply for legalisations and copy certifications. On the date received, please submit your original documents to the TLScontact acceptance centre (address: 6th Floor, Mega Pars Office Complex, Moalem Street, Shariati Avenue, Tehran, Iran – PO Box: 1639769520) and pay the applicable fees (see below). Legalisations Documents of another state are often only recognized by the German authorities or courts if their authenticity has been established in a special procedure. German authorities demand the presentation of Iranian documents e.g. Birth or marriage certificates) often in legalized form. It should be noted that the procedure in Iran is a substitute legalisation procedure. The German Embassy in Tehran is working with the service provider TLScontact to make the implementation of legalisations and copy certifications more customer-friendly. After appointment, TLScontact accepts your documents for the implementation of the replacement legalization procedure or the copy certifications. The documents will continue to be processed only by the German Embassy in Tehran. TLScontact does not carry out a preliminary examination of applications. TLScontact also takes over the return of the documents. Please make an appointment early, especially if you need the legalized documents to present for a visa appointment. Legalization in the visa section on the day of the visa application is no longer possible. You must bring the documents in an already legalized form to your visa appointment. Only public documents issued in Iran can be legalized. The authentic instruments shall include: e.g. Shenasnameh, certificate of integrity, certificate of marriage, certificate of divorce, certificate of death, certificate of death, extract from the land register, business license, carte melli, consent to marriage, ID of the Iranian Medical Association, certificate of diploma, certificate of conduct, car documents, driving license, certificates of notaries such as powers of attorney, affidavit etc. These must necessarily be in the original, otherwise a legalization cannot take place. Please also ensure that the originals (in particular social security certificates, powers of attorney and certificates of conduct of doctors) bear the stamp and signature of the issuing authority. The substitute legalisation is rejected if there is a suspicion that the document is forged or if it is manifestly a false document. Private documents, documents issued by Iranian missions abroad or documents without seal and signature of the exhibitor cannot be legalised. Five steps to substitute legalization in Iran: Translation: You have the public document that you want to legalize translated into German by a translator. Important: The translator must be on the list maintained by the Iranian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://sanam.ekfam.ir/Home/TranslatorList). (Please note that the link can only be accessed on the free Iranian Internet and only on Farsi). Some of them offer to obtain the necessary pre-certifications from the Iranian Ministry of Justice and Foreign Affairs for you. If you accept this offer, you can skip step 2. Pre-certification: They first go to the Iranian Ministry of Justice and then to the Iranian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Department of Legalizations) and have the documents pre-certified there. Please note that the pre-certificates must not be older than one year if you submit them for legalization. Date booking: The appointment is made exclusively through the external service provider TLScontact. For this purpose, an account must be created at TLScontact under the following link: TLScontact registration . After registration, select the “Legalization” category. You will then receive a confirmation email. You are on the waiting list for an appointment. In order to secure the place on the waiting list, a resevation fee of 15,000,000 rial must be paid. Payment shall be made within 48 hours. This re-stay fee shall be reimbursed after the appointment has been made. Further information can be found in the FAQs on the TLS website. As soon as it is your turn, you will be informed separately by email by the external service provider TLScontact. They appear to the booked Date with all necessary documents () Original and pre-certified translations at the acceptance center of TLScontact (Address: 6th Floor, Mega Pars Office Complex, Moalem Street, Shariati Avenue, Tehran, Iran – PO Box: 1639769520). Applications to TLScontact can only be submitted at the Tehran acceptance centre. For the acceptance of the documents to be legalized, TLScontact charges a service fee of 10,- EUR per document. The Fee The message is currently 30,- EUR per document, more information can be found here . Please note that both the embassy fees and the service fee of TLScontact must be paid in cash and in euros on the day of application to TLScontact. You want to hire another person to carry out the procedure for you? The legalization procedure does not necessarily have to be passed personally. You can also hire another person to represent you in this process. Please note the following: The appointment booking is also made in this case with your name and your data. The person authorized by you must bring a written power of attorney in German, English or Farsi signed by you (the person responsible) and a copy of your card Melli to the appointment. Models for the power of attorney in German, English and Persian can be found here: Power of attorney in German Power of attorney in English Power of Persian If the person appearing on TLScontact cannot show this power of attorney and the copy of your card Melli, this person will NOT be admitted. Specificity of Marriage certificates If you want to legalize a marriage certificate, you must present the original Shenasnamehs of both spouses. If you want to legalize a marriage certificate together with a divorce certificate, it is sufficient if a Shenasnameh is presented. Specificity of Divorce certificates If you want to legalize a divorce certificate, you must present at least one Shenasnameh of a former spouse in the original. For the additional required presentation of the Shenasnamehs only as proof, TLScontact does not charge a separate service fee. Specificity of Certificates of conduct : If you want to legalize your Iranian certificate, please make sure it is current (not older than three months). Certification of copies or photographs (copy certification) The German Embassy in Tehran can confirm that a copy is the same as the original. Certificate of copy ), if you need this for German authorities or the German legal sector. A copy of an uncertified copy (simple copy) cannot be certified. If the original is in a language other than German, English or French, a translation into German or English as well as If applicable, a legalization or apostille. Please note that the pre-certificates must not be older than one year if you submit them for copy certification. The copy certification does not confirm the authenticity or correctness of the original document. An appointment is required to apply for copy certifications. See above for replacement legalizations for appointment booking in TLSContact. The cost of the copy certification shall be as follows: The service fee of TLScontact: TLScontact charges EUR 10 Service fee per submitted document from which the copy is to be certified. Should several certifications Same as If the document is required, the service fee is only charged 1x. The Fee The message is currently 25,- EUR per document, more information can be found here . Please note that the above fees must be paid in euros and in cash on the day of application to TLScontact. For students in Germany and for applications for studying in Germany, if appropriate proof of study or the application is a copy certificate for study purposes for: Maximum five sets of copies free of charge. For copy accreditation for other purposes, the general fee rates apply. Please note that the embassy will make the copies for you for a fee. The delivery of self-made copies is not possible. title: List of lawyers and notaries
text: List of lawyers and notaries Law © Colourbox Artikel Here you will find a list of lawyers and notaries working in our district Exclusion of liability This information is based on the message information available at the time of writing. The information and in particular the designation of lawyers and other legal advisers are non-binding and without guarantee. In the case of a mandate, the client has to pay for all costs and fees himself. The appointment of lawyers shall be in alphabetical order. The information on specialties and correspondence languages of the lawyers comes from these themselves and cannot be guaranteed by the embassy. In addition, no claim for completeness is made. General information The Iranian judicial system is complex and the processes are not easy for outsiders to understand. While there is a tendency to increasingly use the judiciary to manage conflicts, the outcome of a trial can still depend to a large extent on factors not only related to the law. e.g. Publicity, societal role of the parties to the proceedings. The process time is difficult to foresee beforehand. Not all lawyers are locally unlimited and admitted in all instances. In addition, there are different types of lawyers: members of the Bar Associations with legal training (“first grade lawyer”, unlimited admission) and lawyers with lower qualifications (“trainee lawyer”, limited admission). A second bar association, which is directly subordinate to the judiciary, was established a few years ago. For civil actions, in contrast to criminal matters, there is a legal obligation. As a benchmark for the attorney’s fee, 7% or 11% are currently required for claims for financial compensation, depending on the amount in dispute. However, the fees can also be freely agreed. Often “Success Fees” are agreed, i.e. Remuneration in case of success. The higher the legal advance, the lower the success fee can be. In principle, the losing party in the dispute shall bear the court costs and expenses, provided that the claimant has submitted a request to the court before the start of the dispute. In the case of an allegation of significant crimes, a compulsory defence lawyer is appointed and there is also the possibility in civil cases to be assigned a lawyer in case of need (via the bar association). As a rule, however, this will not be optimal legal assistance. However, once foreign nationals are involved, legal fee exemptions are unlikely, and in civil cases, foreign nationals are sometimes expected to make advance payments. The local bar associations offer free legal advice on general matters. For questions and concerns concerning the German legal area, German Lawyers and Notaries Further help. title: National visas
text: National visas Artikel Visas for stays over 90 days Choosing the correct visa Preparation of documents Registration on a waiting list Application and personal interview Application processing Request and submission of documents Issuance of the visa Entry Refusal of the visa Important note The Embassy currently operates on a very limited scale. The visa office, the legal and consular department and the external service provider TLScontact work in emergency operations. The message can still be reached via the contact form. Current information on the operation of the embassy can be found here If you have a habitual residence in Iran and intend to stay in Germany for more than 90 days, you must apply for the required visa at the German Embassy in Tehran. Below we will explain the procedure. Choosing the correct visa Before you can apply, clarify which visa you need to apply for. The Visa navigator You can help with that. Preparation of documents Once you know which visa you need, prepare the necessary documents: Blue card EU Visa for research purposes Recognition of qualifications of doctors For the applications of the following visas, a preliminary approval of the Federal Employment Agency or the competent foreigners authority in Accelerated professional procedures according to § 81a AufenthG Required for a visa application: Employment of professionals recognition of vocational training with simultaneous employment; e.g. For nurses Visa for occupational training Visa for marriage with subsequent permanent residence Follow-up to German spouse Follow-up to an underage German child Adherence to a foreign spouse Admission to a minor foreign child Adherence of a minor child to the parent Entrance to another family member Family reunification of Afghan nationals Studies Study project declaration Re-entry Registration on a waiting list Each applicant, including minor children, must make a personal interview with the embassy or external service provider and submit their biometric data and application-based documents. Only after consultation will the visa application be processed. In view of the high demand for consultation appointments, the termination is based on waiting lists. Be sure to register on the waiting list that corresponds to your entry purpose. If you register on a wrong waiting list, your documents cannot be accepted on the agreed date. The waiting lists are processed in chronological order. As soon as it is your turn to make an appointment, you will be contacted by the embassy or the external service provider. Therefore, make sure that you can be reached continuously under the e-mail address specified during registration. Applicants who are nationals of Iran or another state (except Afghanistan), who are habitually resident in Iran and who wish to apply for a visa for the purpose of family reunification or marriage in Germany, register in the Embassy appointment system In principle, a normal stay is to be assumed if a person has already been in Iran for at least six months on the date of application. The waiting list of the message is processed in chronological order. Further information can be found under the link above. applicants with the nationality of Afghanistan, who wish to apply for a visa for the purpose of family reunification or marriage in Germany, register in the Appointment system of the German Embassy in Kabul This also applies to Afghan nationals with habitual residence in Iran. At the moment, when registering on the waiting list of the Embassy Kabul, only Islamabad can be selected as the application location. Registrations of Afghan applicants on the waiting list of the German Embassy Tehran, which were made until 18.06.2025, will continue to be considered. The appointment is made via the external service provider of the embassy TLScontact Since 15. In July 2026, the external service provider released new waiting lists for the purpose of gainful employment. Please only register on the new waiting lists. The external service provider is currently issuing consultation appointments to people who are in the accelerated skilled work process and were already on the waiting list before 18.01.2026. As soon as they have been given the opportunity to submit the application, appointments will also be made for new registrations in the field of the accelerated skilled worker procedure. It is not yet foreseeable when appointments will be made for the other waiting lists in the field of employment. The following new waiting lists exist: NEW National visa: Recognition of qualification for physicians (§16d) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you are a doctor and are seeking recognition of your qualification (usually by attending a specialized language course). Accompanying spouses and minor children must be on the Embassy waiting list for family reunification register. NEW National visa Employment: Fast-Track Medical and Care Proffessions (§81a) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you have prior consent in the accelerated skilled worker procedure (§ 81a) AufenthG ) and work in a medical or nursing profession. Fellow traveling spouses and minor children included in the pre-consent can also register on this waiting list. NEW National visa Employment: Fast-Track Other Proffessions (§81a) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you have prior consent in the accelerated skilled worker procedure (§ 81a) AufenthG ) and work in a profession that is NOT medical in nature. Fellow traveling spouses and minor children included in the pre-consent can also register on this waiting list. NEW National visa Employment: Pre-Approval from Federal Employment Agency Medical and Care Proffessions Register on this waiting list if you have prior consent from the Federal Employment Agency and are working in a medical or nursing profession. Accompanying spouses and minor children can register on this waiting list if the main applicant receives pre-approval for a Blue Card (18g) AufenthG ) has been received. NEW National visa Employment: Pre-Approval from Federal Employment Agency Other Proffessions Register on this waiting list if you have pre-approval from the Federal Employment Agency and are working in a profession that is NOT medical in nature. Accompanying spouses and minor children can register on this waiting list if the main applicant receives pre-approval for a Blue Card (18g) AufenthG ) has been received. NEW National Visa Employment: Blue Card EU (§18g) AufenthG ) without pre-approval Register on this waiting list if you meet the requirements for the Blue Card but have not yet received pre-approval. Fellow traveling spouses and minor children may also register on this waiting list. Applicants who have received prior consent may also register on one of the corresponding waiting lists. NEW National visa researcher Register on this waiting list if you have an admission agreement pursuant to §18d AufenthG received from a German research institution. Fellow traveling spouses and minor children may also register on this waiting list. The appointment is made via the external service provider of the embassy TLScontact Since 15. In July 2026, the external service provider released new waiting lists for the purpose of studying. Please only register on the new waiting lists. Applicants who are already registered on an old waiting list will be given the option of priority termination as soon as new appointments can be made. However, you can also register on one of the new waiting lists. Due to limited capacities, it is currently not yet foreseeable when new consultation appointments will be allocated based on the old or new waiting lists. This also applies to students who want to study from the winter semester 2026/2027. The following new waiting lists exist: NEW National visa: Student Master/ PhD (§16b) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you have received an admission certificate from a German university and have received a master's or PhD -Aspire to study or if you will attend a degree program that graduates with a state exam. Accompanying spouses and minor children must be on the Embassy waiting list for family reunification register. Exception: minor children and spouses of applicants who are a DAAD - Scholarship received and which are also included in the scholarship can register on this waiting list. NEW National visa students: Bachelor (§16b) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you have received an admission certificate from a German university and are looking for a Bachelor scholarship. Accompanying spouses and minor children must be on the Embassy waiting list for family reunification register. NEW National visa students: Studienkolleg (§16b) AufenthG ) and Preparatory language course (16b) AufenthG ) Register on this waiting list if you have been accepted to a Studienkolleg or to take a preparatory language course. If you need a visa for re-entry, please first register on our Waiting list . After successful registration, you will receive a confirmation email with your reference number within 30 minutes. Two weeks before the actual appointment, you will receive an appointment mail with the date and time of your appointment. The dates are allocated strictly on time. In your confirmation email with details of your appointment, you will find a link with which you can cancel or confirm your appointment. If you want to make the appointment, we ask you to confirm this via the link within 7 days of receiving the mail. Otherwise, you will not be admitted to the visa section on the day of the specified date. We also urge you to cancel the appointment if you are no longer interested in the appointment or. have visas or are otherwise prevented on this date. Due to limited capacity, the Embassy is currently unable to set regular dates for applying for a visa for the purpose of re-entry. In individual cases, the embassy will issue special appointments if you are already registered on the waiting list and have the pre-approval of the immigration authority for re-entry. Apply in such a case visainfo@tehe.diplo.de and send a copy of the pre-consent and your registration number on the waiting list. If you wish to apply for a national visa that is NOT for family reunification or re-entry, and if you do not fit into any other of the above categories in the field of study or employment, please register on the following waiting list through the external service provider of the Embassy TLScontact NEW National visa Other (e.g. Opportunity Card); Employment without pre-approval; au pair; NO family reunion’ Accompanying family members register on the Embassy waiting list for family reunification . Applicants who require a re-entry visa register on the waiting list of the embassy for the purpose of re-entry. Due to limited capacities, there are currently no appointments for this waiting list. It is not yet clear when appointments can begin. Application and personal interview You usually submit the visa application at the personal appointment, which will be assigned to you after registration on the waiting list. In the area of family reunification, it may be necessary to submit the application informally before personal consultation in order to meet deadlines. Information on when this may be necessary and how to proceed in such a case can be found here As part of the personal interview, you provide your biometric data, submit your application-based documents and pay the visa fee. You will then receive a receipt with your processing number. Application processing The processing of your application begins after a personal consultation, submission of the biometric data and submission of the application-based documents. Applications in the field of family reunification are processed by the visa section of the Embassy of Tehran. Applications in the field of study and skilled immigration are submitted by the Federal Office for Foreign Affairs (Federal Office for Foreign Affairs). BfAA ) processed. After consultation, check the message or BfAA the existence of the granting requirements and, where necessary, participate in domestic authorities (Federal Employment Agency or Aliens Authority). The processing period depends on the purpose of the stay, the completeness of the documents and the feedback from the domestic authorities. Request and submission of documents Provides the The message or the BfAA During the processing of your application, it is determined that your documents are incomplete or that additional documents are necessary in your individual case, you will be asked to submit additional documents. The form and way in which the documents are to be presented depends on the individual case. You will receive detailed instructions along with the request. For this reason, make sure that you can also be reached during the processing procedure via the contact data provided at the time of application. If They themselves want to submit additional documents after a personal interview because your circumstances have changed () e.g. Relocation of the reference person in Germany; change your employment contract; Provide a missing language, please do the following: Send an email to visainfo@teheran.diplo.de and attach a scan of the supplementary document in PDF format to the e-mail as an attachment. In the subject and text of the email, be sure to mention your processing number, your name and date of birth and explain why you are sending the document. You will then receive a confirmation of the message about the access to the document. If the original of the document is required, you will be asked separately. Do not send unsolicited original documents by post and do not throw originals unsolicited into the mailbox of the embassy. Issuance of the visa Your visa will be granted if you meet the granting requirements and the domestic authorities have given their consent where necessary. Once your visa is ready for issuance, you will be asked by the embassy or external service provider to submit your passport for sighting or to audition together with the passport. You will receive detailed instructions along with the request. Information on the presentation and collection of your passport by an authorised representative can be found here* * The national visa is usually issued in the area of family reunification for the first 90 days of stay. Visas in the field of study and skilled immigration are usually granted for the first year of stay. Entry The national visa entitles you to enter Germany and to enter the purpose of stay indicated in the visa label. First entry is possible via any Schengen state. The national visa also entitles you to stay in the other Schengen states during the period of 90 days each 180 days. However, the national visa does not guarantee entry. The final decision on whether you will be granted entry is made by the border authorities. Therefore, make sure that you also carry with you all documents that you have submitted for the visa application (in particular declaration of obligation, proof of the purpose of stay, proof of travel health insurance). If your visa contains a cash requirement, you must be able to carry the required amount in cash and show it on request. Refusal of the visa If you do not meet the eligibility requirements, your visa application will be rejected. You will receive a rejection letter. The rejection notice will be handed over to you in personal consultation or announced by e-mail. The refusal decision explains the reasons for the refusal. Against the refusal of the visa, only the action before the Administrative Court Berlin is admissible. Additional information on the complaint procedure can be obtained from here Alternatively, after rejecting your application, you can New visa application pose. However, they have to go through the entire application process again, including entry on the waiting list, personal interview and presentation of all the supporting documents. The remonstration procedure has been abolished since 01.07.2025. Requests for remonstrations against rejection orders issued after 01.07.2025 will not be processed. text: Nationality Naturalisation certificate © dpa/pa 01.02.2022 - Artikel German citizenship law is complex and has been changed frequently in recent years. The Federal Office of Administration shall provide up-to-date information on its website. Website available. Through this website you can Among others also on the decision of the Federal Constitutional Court of 20.05.2020, by which the circle of beneficiaries from Article 116 paragraph 2 sentence 1 of the Basic Law GG ) has been extended, inform. Descendants of Nazi persecuted persons can more easily obtain German citizenship: The Federal Ministry of the Interior, for Building and Homeland (German). BMI has put into effect two comprehensive decree regulations, the descendants of German Nazi persecuted living abroad, who are not entitled to re-naturalization under Article 116 (2) of the Basic Law ( GG ) to facilitate naturalization. We want you with this Information leaflet in particular point to an aspect of nationality law, according to which in individual cases no automatic acquisition of German nationality by birth takes place; so-called intergenerational. On 20 August 2021, the Fourth Law amending the Nationality Act came into force, creating a statutory right to restitution naturalisation for persons who were affected by National Socialist persecution measures and therefore lost or did not acquire German citizenship, but no right to re-acquire German citizenship in accordance with Art. 116, par. 2, first sentence GG and for their descendants. The law also includes a ten-year right of declaration which opens up the possibility of obtaining German citizenship by simple declaration to children born after the entry into force of the Basic Law of a German parent who were excluded from obtaining German citizenship in a gender-discriminatory manner on the basis of the regulations in force at the time of their birth. The Fourth Act amending the Nationality Act, which entered into force on 20.08.2021, creates a ten-year right of declaration (§ 5 StAG) which, after 23.05.1949 (entry into force of the Basic Law), opens up the possibility of obtaining German nationality by means of a simple declaration to the competent nationality authority. The possibility of acquiring a declaration also exists for their descendants. Beneficiaries include persons born after 23.05.1949 children of a German parent who have not acquired German citizenship by birth (children of a German mother and a foreign father born before 01.01.1975 and children of a German father and a foreign mother not born before 01.07.1993), children of a mother who lost German nationality before the birth of the child by marriage to a foreigner pursuant to § 17 No. 6 RuStAG a.F. before 01.04.1953, children who have lost their German citizenship acquired by birth through a legitimation effected by a foreigner and effective under German laws in accordance with § 17 no. 5 RuStAG a.F. before 01.04.1953, and descendants of the children referred to in points 1 to 3. Persons who have their habitual residence abroad may make the declaration directly to the Federal Office of Administration or to the competent mission abroad. It shall take effect upon receipt by the competent nationality authority () i.e. in the case of residence abroad at the Federal Office of Administration, if the other conditions are met. Detailed information on the acquisition of the declaration and the relevant forms can be found on the Website of the Federal Office of Administration . text: Pension matters Older couple walking © colorbox.com 01.12.2017 - Artikel Pension matters should usually be clarified directly by the pension recipient with the respective German pension carrier. There is no mandatory participation of the embassy. Missions abroad may only provide general information. You should discuss specific questions about your pension application directly with the case officer of your pension insurance. In addition, however, it often happens in pension matters that official confirmations and certificates are required. These can be issued at the embassy during opening hours at the legal and consular counter (Sunday to Thursday between 07:30 and 10:30). The issue of the certificate is free of charge for statutory pension insurance. This also applies to: Legalisations documents requested by pension insurance institutions. Certificates of life Life certificates are usually requested by pension insurance providers in Germany once a year. Please bring to the embassy the certificate of life form sent by the pension insurance provider and your current passport as proof of identity. If you receive a widow's pension and have Iranian citizenship, the presentation of your Shenasnameh is also necessary. A certificate of life can only be Personal presentation be granted. Even with a long journey or illness, you cannot be spared a personal visit to the embassy. You then send the life certificate together with the form to the pension insurance institution. If the pension insurance form sent by post has been lost, you can obtain a certificate of life on a form available here at the embassy. However, please keep your social security number ready for this case, as the life certificate cannot be assigned otherwise in Germany. text: Prevention and management of sexual harassment Artikel The Federal Foreign Office, with more than 220 missions and employees from more than 150 countries, stands for lived diversity. title: Prevention of corruption
text: Prevention of corruption Corruption prevention © colourbox Artikel The Federal Administration’s Corruption Prevention Directive of 30 July 2004 defines the legal framework for the tasks of corruption prevention at the Federal Foreign Office headquarters and at missions abroad. Prevention of corruption The Federal Administration’s Corruption Prevention Directive of 30 July 2004 defines the legal framework for the tasks of corruption prevention at the Federal Foreign Office headquarters and at missions abroad. The RL aims to provide, in an understandable manner and by means of fewer regulations, a guideline for administrative action that is inclusive, fair and transparent. Ethical principles and the rejection of corruption should be firmly anchored in the consciousness of the employees at the Federal Foreign Office headquarters and the seconded employees and local staff at the missions abroad. Important concerns in the prevention of corruption at the Federal Foreign Office headquarters and at the missions abroad are raising employees’ awareness of the topic, protecting them from possible risks of corruption and maintaining high ethical and legal standards as an integral part of all administrative activities. Rewards, personal gifts or other benefits The principle applies: prohibition of adoption! Employees at the Federal Foreign Office headquarters, as well as seconded staff and local staff at missions abroad, may not accept rewards, gifts or other benefits related to their office without the permission of the department. All other regulations can be found in the circular of the BMI on the prohibition on the acceptance of rewards or gifts in the Federal Administration of 8 November 2004 and the relevant circulars at the Federal Foreign Office. Further hints and answers to frequently asked questions and under the guidance of the BMI elaborated by the Initiative Group for the Prevention of Corruption between the Federal Administration and business Catalogue of questions and answers on the subject of acceptance of rewards, gifts or other benefits (gifts). Questions and advice on corruption prevention at the Federal Foreign Office can also be addressed to the Commissioner for Corruption Prevention at the Federal Foreign Office. title: Procedure following refusal of a visa application
text: Procedure following refusal of a visa application Artikel The remonstration procedure was abolished with effect from 01.07.2025. If you have received a negative decision from the embassy since 01.07.2025, the decision cannot be reviewed again by the embassy. Requests for re-examination of a negative decision received by the embassy will no longer be processed. You have the option to reject the decision. Action to be submitted. The binding appeal policy in your refusal decision indicates that you have brought the action before the Administrative Court of Berlin as a competent body within a One month from the date of notification of the decision can collect. You can hire a lawyer to bring an action; However, there is no obligation to do so. For the assignment of a lawyer, there are usually separate lawyer fees. The court language is German (§ 184 Judicial Constitution Act). The court proceedings shall be charged. The amount of the court fees is determined by the court. The fees are usually due as an advance immediately after the action has been brought. The amount depends on the number of desired visas. For a procedure, a Court fee of approximately 480 euros collected. Legal aid can be applied for in court and is regularly granted only if there is a chance of success in the direct action proceedings. Questions about costs and cost regulations can only be answered by the administrative court. The exact duration and course of the court proceedings shall be determined by the court. Under special circumstances, the court proceedings may be accelerated by urgent procedure. There are separate court fees for this. Further information on administrative disputes in visa matters, in particular on costs, can be found on the Website of the Administrative Court Berlin Leaflet of the Administrative Court Berlin on visa dispute procedures Information on the procedure and costs of the direct action procedure shall be non-binding and shall not be entitled to completeness. The Embassy cannot advise you on the course of the proceedings and also on whether the action is expedient in individual cases. Please contact a lawyer or directly the Administrative Court of Berlin. Alternatively, after rejecting your application, you can New visa application Identify . However, they have to go through the entire application process again, including entry on the waiting list, personal interview and presentation of all the supporting documents. title: Procurement of civil status documents in Germany
text: Procurement of civil status documents in Germany Registry © dpa Artikel Civil status documents () e.g. Birth certificates or marriage certificates can be applied for in Germany at the registry office where the civil status case was certified. To obtain civil status certificates from Germany, you must therefore contact the competent registry office. Registers for communal districts are usually kept by the registry office of the higher municipality. If the civil status case has occurred outside the Federal Republic of Germany and the corresponding event has been certified at the Registry Office I in Berlin, civil status certificates must be requested at the Registry Office I in Berlin. Further information and corresponding application forms can be found on the Website of the Standesamt I Berlin. Info Civil status certificates must be applied for in person by the applicant at the competent German Registry Office. The mission abroad cannot therefore take over the request/procurement for you. Model request to the registry office for a birth certificate: ‘I ask for a birth certificate for ... (first name(s)/surname), If applicable, Name at birth, born on ... (date of birth) in ... (place of birth). Parents: ... (father’s name), born on ... in ... and ... (mother’s birth name) born on ... in ... Please write out the month of your birth date in letters, e.g. 10 December 1956. The fees are set directly by the registry office and usually have to be transferred to a German account. More information can be obtained from your registry office. title: Proof of language proficiency in the visa procedure
text: Proof of language proficiency in the visa procedure 28.04.2026 - Artikel Language proficiency requirement for spouse reunification Exceptions to the requirement of language skills in the case of spouse reunification Language skills requirements for child reunification Exemptions from the requirement of language skills for child reunification Language proficiency requirement for spouse reunification Adherence to the spouse usually presupposes that the following foreigner has simple language skills on the Level A1 is available. Proof of simple language skills by presenting a ALTE-certified language diploma At the time of application, the language certificate may: not older than 1 year. In certain constellations, a legal exception to the language requirement applies. In addition, in certain cases, proof of sufficient language skills may be waived. Below you will find a list of the most common exceptions. If you are unsure whether you need to provide the language proof, contact the embassy in good time. The final decision as to whether language proof can be waived in your case is made by the embassy in agreement with the competent foreigners authority in Germany only after the personal interview has taken place and the application-based documents have been submitted in full. Exceptions to the requirement of language skills in the case of spouse reunification The requirement of simple language skills does not apply if the spouse holds one of the following residence permits in Germany and the marriage already existed when the spouse moved his center of life to Germany in Germany: Residence permit according to § 23 (4) AufenthG Residence permit according to § 25 (1) or (2) AufenthG Establishment permit according to § 26 paragraph 3 AufenthG Establishment permit according to § 26 paragraph 4 AufenthG after a residence permit in accordance with § 25 (2) sentence 1 second alternative existed The requirement of simple language skills does not apply if the spouse holds or has held one of the following residence permits in Germany before immediately obtaining a residence permit or permanent residence permit: EU has been granted: Blue card EU ICT Card or mobile ICT - Card Residence permit according to §§ 18a, 18b, 18c paragraph 3, §§ 18d, 18f AufenthG Residence permit referred to in 19c(1) AufenthG for employment as a manager, manager, company specialist, scientist, guest scientist, engineer or technician in the research team of a guest scientist or teacher Residence permit according to § 19c paragraph 2 or 4 sentence 1 AufenthG Residence permit according to § 21 AufenthG The requirement of simple language skills does not apply if the reunification takes place not only to the spouse, but also to the minor German child for whom you have custody. The proof of simple language skills is not required if there are language skills that are obviously above level A1. Obviousness shall be established at the personal interview. This may include: e.g. Persons who have already stayed in Germany in the past or have worked in a German-speaking environment. In case of doubt about the obviousness, the message may request a language certificate. The requirement for simple language skills is eliminated with a noticeably low integration requirement This shall be presumed if the trainee has a professional qualification: and a positive employment forecast and positive integration prognosis. This presupposes that the inclusion of a professional activity corresponding to the qualification in the federal territory within a reasonable period of time and the integration into social life in Germany are realistic. This may include: e.g. if there is sufficient knowledge of another European language. The requirement of simple language skills is waived if the trainee has already stayed permanently in the federal territory in the past and was entitled to an integration course during this stay. The previous stay must be one of the requirements of § 44 Abs. 1 AufenthG the abovementioned purposes. The proof of simple language skills shall not be required in the following cases: Due to a physical, mental or mental illness or disability, the following spouse is unable to acquire simple knowledge of the German language. In such a case, you will need to attach meaningful evidence to your application that proves your physical/mental/mental limitation. The embassy may insist on an examination by a trusted physician. Advanced age or illiteracy are not in themselves sufficient exceptional grounds. Due to special circumstances of the individual case, it is not possible or unreasonable for the following spouse to make efforts to acquire a simple knowledge of the German language or to provide proof of existing language skills before entering the country. In the course of at least one year, despite serious, targeted efforts, the following spouse has failed to obtain proof of sufficient language skills. In such a case, you must provide the application with meaningful evidence of your language acquisition efforts () e.g. Certificates of participation in language courses and exams, exercise booklets, communications of results of exams taken Language skills requirements for child reunification When a child joins the custodial parent, under certain circumstances, the presentation of a language certificate on the Level C1 required. The proof shall be furnished by a ALTE-certified language diploma provided. Language skills shall be required if: the child at the time of application is 16. has completed the year of life AND a custodial parent or both custodial parents are already in Germany AND the child submits his application for family reunification not in connection with the departure of the parent already living in Germany. As a rule, a period of 6 months is considered a chronological relationship. Information on what constitutes an application and how you can maintain the age limit in case of long waiting times can be found here. In certain constellations, a legal exception to the language requirement applies. Below you will find a list of the most common exceptions. If you are unsure whether your child needs to provide the language proof, contact the message in time. The final decision as to whether the language proof can be dispensed with is made by the embassy in agreement with the competent foreigners authority in Germany only after the personal interview has taken place and the documents justifying the application have been submitted in full. Exemptions from the requirement of language skills for child reunification The requirement of simple language skills does not apply if the parent holds one of the following residence permits in Germany: Residence permit according to § 23 (4) AufenthG Residence permit according to § 25 (1) or (2) AufenthG Establishment permit according to § 26 paragraph 3 AufenthG Establishment permit according to § 26 paragraph 4 AufenthG after a residence permit pursuant to § 25 (2) sentence 1 second alternative existed. The requirement of simple language skills shall not apply if the parent living in Germany or his/her spouse living with him/her in a family relationship holds or has held one of the following residence permits before he/she immediately received a residence permit or permanent residence permit: EU has been granted: Blue card EU ICT Card or mobile ICT - Card Residence permit according to §§ 18a, 18b, 18c paragraph 3, §§ 18d, 18f AufenthG Residence permit referred to in 19c(1) AufenthG for employment as a manager, manager, company specialist, scientist, guest scientist, engineer or technician in the research team of a guest scientist or as a teacher. Residence permit according to § 19c paragraph 2 or 4 sentence 1 AufenthG Residence permit according to § 21 AufenthG The language requirement may be waived if it is necessary due to the circumstances of the individual case in order to avoid a particular hardship. The best interests of the child and the family situation are taken into account. Does the child or if the parent claims such particular hardship, they must substantiate the existence of such hardship in the application process and provide appropriate evidence. A final decision as to whether the language requirement can be waived is made by the embassy in agreement with the competent foreigners authority only after consultation and presentation of all documents justifying the application. title: Restricted operation of the message
text: Restricted operation of the message 09.03.2026 - Artikel Legal and Consular Department Visa office Passports and other original documents Application processing and status consultation Processing times Important note Due to the current situation, the embassy and in particular the consulate and visa office work in an emergency operation. The visa section will remain closed for regular visitors until further notice. There are no regular visa appointments yet. The external service provider of the embassy, TLScontact, also works in restricted operation. Since 15 July 2026, registration on new waiting lists of the external service provider is possible again. However, TLScontact does not yet award new appointments in all categories. Further information can be found below. There is currently no reliable indication as to when the regular operation of the visa section can be resumed. However, the message can still be reached via contact form. Please DO NOT travel unsolicited to neighboring countries for visa applications. Their applications are rejected there due to lack of competence and are not processed. Legal and Consular Department Passport office and consular certificates German nationals who need help in the current situation can contact the Standby telephone or contact form report. If your German travel document has expired, please contact Contact form to the Legal and Consular Department. Applications for legalization of Iranian documents are made through the service provider TLScontact. Applications are currently accepted by people who have registered on the waiting list before January 2026. We inform here as soon as the waiting list for new registrations is opened again. Dates for signature certifications and consular certificates are offered again. You can make an appointment here booking. All other consular services are currently not available. Visa office The message is aware of the difficult situation of applicants and strives to offer individual solutions within the capacities and possibilities. The embassy contacts those applicants who can be offered alternative options. Schengen visas: The German Embassy in Tehran remains solely responsible for processing short-stay visa applications (Schengen visas) for Iranian nationals legally residing in Iran. This also applies in cases where Iranian nationals legally residing in Iran are temporarily present in third countries. Due to the temporary closure of the visa section, it is currently not possible to accept new applications. Please refrain from travelling to neighbouring countries for visa applications. Their applications are rejected there due to lack of competence and are not processed. The Embassy is currently making appointments in individual cases via the external service provider TLScontact in humanitarian or medical emergencies in which timely entry is urgently required. Entries to participate in family celebrations () e.g. Wedding) or the birth of the grandchild are not considered emergencies. Since July 15, registration on the new waiting list for obtaining a Schengen visa via the external service provider TLScontact is again possible. However, it is not yet foreseeable when new appointments will be made for these waiting lists. People who were already on the external service provider’s old waiting list will be given the opportunity to make appointments as soon as new appointments can be made. However, you can also register on the new waiting list. Applications that have already been submitted will be processed successively. Schengen visas already issued, which cannot be used due to the crisis situation, cannot be extended or renewed by the embassy. In these cases, new visa applications must be submitted under the regular procedure after resumption of service. Applicants who have been able to leave with a valid Schengen visa but cannot travel back to Iran due to the airspace closure, please contact the foreigners authority at their place of residence. National visas of Iranian nationals (family reunification): The German Embassy in Tehran continues to be responsible for processing visa applications of Iranian nationals residing in Iran for the purpose of family reunification. The waiting list for Iranian nationals for the purpose of family reunification is further processed to a limited extent. Please note the following : Consultation appointments are only granted by prior agreement and in chronological order of registrations. For this purpose, you will be informed by the International Organization for Migration, Family Assistance Programme (Family Assistance Programme). IOM FAP) contacted unsolicited as soon as it is your turn to register. The application shall be made by means of: IOM FAP office in Istanbul. Important: Please do not make appointment registrations at the Consulate General Istanbul or iDATA. Only those who have registered on the waiting list of the Embassy of Tehran will be considered. Persons who had already been given appointments in January 2026 and who were unable to attend due to the closure of the embassy will also be given the opportunity, in accordance with existing capacities, to: IOM FAP Istanbul office. This group of people will also be contacted by IOPM FAP Istanbul to prepare the presentation. Requests from persons who have already spoken to the Embassy of Tehran or the external service provider TLScontact will be further processed within the capacity of the Embassy. Visas ready for issuance are issued within the framework of the existing capacities in Tehran. Applicants who have already been asked to collect your visa in Yerevan can collect your visa in either Yevan or Tehran. Please contact the embassy via visainfo@tehe.diplo.de. Please refrain from speaking to other missions abroad on your own initiative to submit your application or collect your visa. This will not speed up your procedure. Coordination continues through the Embassy of Tehran and the International Organization for Migration. IOM ) A meeting at another embassy outside Iran may only take place upon request. National visas in the accelerated skilled labour process: TLScontact is currently awarding new appointments to persons who have received advance approval in the accelerated specialist procedure according to § 81a AufenthG have; and - who were on a waiting list of the external service provider at the time of 18.01.2026 OR - whose already agreed meeting was cancelled due to the closure of the embassy from 16.01.2026. TLScontact contacts the affected parties by telephone. Applicants who continue to have a current employment contract and a declaration of employment with beginning in the future, as well as any other Required supporting documents have the opportunity to submit their biometric data and request-reasoning documents to the TLScontact acceptance centre. Family members (spouse and minor children) are given the opportunity to audition at the same time as the main applicant. TLScontact will also accept the application documents if the actual prior consent has already expired. TLScontact will accept the application documents of the spouses of the main applicants even if the required language proof is older than one year. Family members who have to present legalized documents will be given the opportunity to submit the documents for the purpose of legalization at the same time as the application documents for the visa, as long as the documents are at least pre-certified. Applicants whose application files are outdated shall be given the opportunity to update them within one month. If applicants do not have sufficiently up-to-date application documents even after the end of the month, they must register on the new waiting list. Applications are accepted as usual via the Federal Foreign Office (Federal Office for Foreign Affairs). BfAA ) processed. The issue-ready visas are handed over via the Embassy in Tehran within the framework of existing capacities. Applicants in the accelerated skilled labour process who are not yet on a waiting list can apply since the 15th. Register July 2026 on one of the new waiting lists of the external service provider. The processing of the new waiting list starts within the existing capacities as soon as all applicants who were on the old waiting list have been given the opportunity to submit their application documents. Applicants who were on the old waiting list and have not yet been contacted by TLScontact can also register on the new waiting list. National visas (study, employment, researchers, doctors; no accelerated skilled worker procedure): The German Embassy in Tehran and the Federal Office for Foreign Affairs continue to be responsible for processing visa applications of Iranian and Afghan nationals residing in Iran for one of the above-mentioned purposes. Requests from persons who have already spoken to the embassy or the external service provider will be further processed within the capacity of the embassy. Visas ready for issuance will be issued at the Tehran Embassy within the framework of existing capacities. Since July 15, applicants in the above categories can register on one of the new waiting lists of the external service provider. However, it is not yet foreseeable when new appointments for these waiting lists can be allocated. This also applies to students who want to start their studies in WS 2026/27. Please refrain from speaking to other missions abroad on your own initiative to submit your application or collect your visa. This will not speed up your procedure. Coordination continues through the Embassy of Tehran or the Federal Foreign Office. A meeting at another embassy outside Iran may only take place upon request. National visas of Afghan nationals (family reunification): Due to the temporary closure of the visa section, it is currently not possible to accept new applications. The Federal Foreign Office is currently examining alternative ways of accepting applications for people who are already on the waiting list of Tehran or Kabul embassies with Tehran as the place of application. As soon as reliable information is available, corresponding information is published on the website and the affected persons are contacted individually. Requests from persons who have already spoken to the Embassy or the external service provider will be further processed by the Embassy of Tehran or the Federal Office for Foreign Affairs within the framework of the capacities. Visas ready for issuance will be issued within the framework of existing capacities by the Embassy of Tehran. Please refrain from speaking to other missions abroad on your own initiative to submit your application or collect your visa. This will not speed up your procedure. Coordination continues through the Embassy of Tehran or the Federal Foreign Office. A meeting at another embassy outside Iran may only take place upon request. Passports and other original documents Passports of applicants who have handed in their passports to the external service provider TLScontact or the embassy and have not yet received them back will remain with the service provider or the embassy, depending on the individual case. The documents are safely stored there and will be handed over to the affected persons as soon as the operation of the service provider / embassy could be resumed. The embassy contacts the affected applicants without being asked. The processing of the respective application will also continue without the passport. If the issuance of the visa is in principle possible in individual cases, but there is still no access to the already submitted passport, there is the possibility of issuing the visa in a new passport. The details will be individually coordinated by the embassy with those affected. The same applies to other originals, such as civil status certificates or certificates. These documents, if they have not yet been handed over, remain with the embassy. The message is successively connected with the affected persons and individually coordinates restitution options. Application processing and status consultation The current processing status of applications submitted via the external service provider TLScontact can be viewed again via the TLS portal. Please refrain from telephone inquiries and e-mails about the processing status, as these can additionally delay the processes. I notify the embassy unsolicited to the applicants, as far as they need information in the visa procedure. Processing times Due to the current situation, greatly extended processing times are to be expected in all areas. The competent authorities continue to work hard to deal with all issues as quickly as possible. We ask for your understanding of the current inconvenience and thank you for your patience and cooperation. text: Studies with admission to a university in Germany Artikel Basic conditions Documents to be submitted Here you will find answers to your questions Basic conditions In order to apply for a visa to study at a German university, you must in any case meet the following requirements: You need an unconditional or under certain conditions granted admission to study. You can prove sufficient financial means to secure your livelihood at least for the first year of stay. If you enter to study, your livelihood is considered secure if you receive a sum of €992 or Per academic year € 11,904 (as of 2026) available. If your university requires tuition, you must prove to us that you can also bear these costs in the first year. You must prove that you have sufficient language skills. An overview of the recognized language evidence can be found here You can also prove sufficient language skills if you have one of the following educational degrees: the German Abitur, the German Language Certificate of Level II of the KMK DSD -II, the Central High Level Test (ZOP) of the GI, the Small German Language Certificate (KDS) of the GI or the Great German Language Certificate (GDS) of the GI If the language of instruction of your degree programme is English, you must ensure that the language skills required for your studies are available on the basis of language certificates from recognised institutions. e.g. IELTS, TOEFL. title: Succession
text: Succession Decline of an inheritance © Colourbox Artikel Have you become an heir and is there an estate in Germany? Read here how you can apply for an inheritance certificate or reject the inheritance. General information In German law, the estate of a deceased person passes directly to the heir or heirs. To prove who has become an heir, the certificate of inheritance is issued by the competent estate court in Germany at the request of an heir or several co-heirs. By applying for a certificate of inheritance, the heir indicates that he has accepted the inheritance. However, in some cases, such as when the estate is over-indebted, it may be advisable to reject the inheritance after the decedent. For this purpose, the heir must declare to the estate court within a certain period that he rejects the inheritance. Whoever does not reject the inheritance within the time limit has become an inheritance. Certificate of inheritance A certificate of inheritance is not necessary in every death. If a notarially opened will is available, for example, no separate certificate of inheritance is required. As a rule, landowners, banks and sometimes also pension authorities demand in the case of inheritance before rewriting or payment of a certificate of heirship. This applies regardless of whether the deceased was a German or a foreign national. Please first clarify with the authorities or banks involved whether a certificate of inheritance is required in your individual case. The request for an inheritance certificate is submitted to the competent probate court, which is in most cases the district court in whose district the deceased had his last residence. In the case of several heirs, the application for a (Community) certificate of inheritance by one heir alone is sufficient if it indicates that the other heirs have accepted the inheritance. In addition, the application for an inheritance certificate may be submitted by an authorised lawyer. As far as is necessary in individual cases, the certification of the affidavit can be made in the embassy after the certificate of inheritance has already been applied for by the competent estate court in Germany. In this case, please contact the Contact form . There are longer waiting times during processing. Loss of inheritance According to German law, an inheritance can be rejected within six weeks, if the heir stays outside Germany or if the deceased’s only last residence outside Germany within six months after knowledge of the succession by declaration to the German court of succession. The declaration shall be made at least in a publicly certified form. Parents who act as custodians of their minor children must both declare the inheritance tax on their child. If you are habitually resident in the Islamic Republic of Iran, the declaration of invalidity can be made before a consular officer of the German Embassy in Tehran. Please book one for this Signature authentication date and bring the letter sent by the probate court filled in but not yet signed to the appointment. title: Timely application
text: Timely application 28.04.2026 - Artikel The timely application is necessary if the entitlement to receive a visa depends on the application being made before a certain deadline has been reached and at the same time, due to long waiting times for an appointment, you do not have the opportunity to speak personally to the embassy within the deadline to submit your application. First and foremost, this concerns underage children joining a parent or Parents who become a minor refugee follow suit. If a deadline or age limit is approaching that you or your minor child must meet and no appointment has been made at the embassy, please proceed as follows: Write a request for family reunification from the child or family members. The application does not have to take a specific form, but it must include: - Personal data of the applicant child / relatives (surname, first name, date of birth) - personal data of the reference person, to which the follow-up should be - Application request (receiving the visa) and Reason for request (i.e. family reunification to the child/father/mother/spouse) Sign the application by hand. Adult applicants must sign the application themselves. Applications from children should in the best case be signed by both parents. Send a scan of this request by email Amount visainfo@tehe.diplo.de with the subject ‘timely application’. They will then receive a Acknowledgement of receipt of the application by e-mail. Keep your request, email and receipt carefully. Once you or your child has been assigned an appointment, a Printing of the informal request, the e-mail sent to the embassy and the acknowledgement of receipt by the embassy necessarily together with all other documents.
